## What you are gating
This is a DESIGN gate (no code yet). Rule on the design + ANSWER the 7 design questions so the
build is correct first time. The full grounded forensic is at `.codex/I-perm-021/forensic.md`
(read it — it cites file:line). The issue is #1213: build a RequiredEntityLedger so the report
INCLUDES all verified required entities and DISCLOSES unsupported ones as explicit evidence_gaps
(never hallucinated); gap-driven targeted retrieval for missing slots; default-OFF flag,
byte-identical when off; faithfulness gates (strict_verify/4-role/D8) NEVER relaxed.

## The forensic's load-bearing findings (verify these)
1. **The completeness gap is LARGELY UPSTREAM (retrieval/extraction), not reduce-time.** A
   reduce-time ledger cannot MANUFACTURE coverage — it can only force INCLUSION of already-verified
   findings + DISCLOSE the rest + FEED bounded retrieval. If this is wrong, say so.
2. POLARIS ALREADY measures required-entity coverage post-strict_verify, report-level
   (`native_gate_b_inputs.build_native_gate_b_inputs` :649-783, `_claim_covers_entity` :514-535,
   `_entity_canonical_match` exact-equality :293-309). `missing = required − ⋃covered` is already
   computable there. ALREADY has a bounded gap-retrieval lane (`run_required_entity_lane` #1190,
   required_entity_retrieval.py:295-428, wired run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4808, default OFF) — but
   S0-safety-only, fires once pre-gen, never closes the loop.
3. **The owner's proposed hook (`_run_section`, between distillate and reduce) is at the WRONG
   ALTITUDE:** the S1 pivotal-trial entities (the bulk of the clinical denominator) render via the
   V30 CONTRACT path (`contract_section_runner.py`), not `_run_section`; and `_run_section` is dark
   unless `PG_SECTION_DISTILL` is also on. `generate_multi_section_report` (:4711) receives NO
   template/slug/required_entities. So a `_run_section`-local ledger is structurally blind to S1.
4. **Sequencing contradiction:** "ledger BEFORE reduce" + "missing = required − VERIFIED bindings" +
   "strict_verify is the SOLE VERIFIED authority" cannot all hold (strict_verify runs on the reduce
   OUTPUT). Forensic resolves with a TWO-PHASE ledger: Phase A (pre-gen, corpus-presence → MAPPED,
   drives extended pre-gen retrieval), Phase B (post-strict_verify, VERIFIED/INCLUDED/GAP_DISCLOSED).
5. The V30 contract path ALREADY discloses unfillable trial slots faithfully
   (`contract_section_runner.py:993-1019` + `compose_gap_payload` :370-372) — the evidence_gaps
   precedent. Gap disclosures are DETERMINISTIC TEMPLATED strings (no LLM), never fabricated prose.

## §-1.1 faithfulness design (the crux — verify every path)
INCLUDED reachable ONLY from VERIFIED; VERIFIED set ONLY by reading strict_verify is_verified=True
(ledger never sets is_verified). Gap retrieval never keys a fetched row to an entity_id (the #1190
invariant :319) and coverage still requires EXACT canonical match (alt URL can't flip a url_pattern
entity — recovery gets CONTENT in, never forces coverage). evidence_gaps are templated, no fabricated
citation. Re-generation (if any) re-runs the UNCHANGED strict_verify. The ledger MUST NOT add a new
D8 coverage-CREDIT path (its VERIFIED set must EQUAL `_claim_covers_entity`); new credit = §-1.1-lethal.
Native scope template is the ONLY source of "required" (the contamination lock — NEVER read
outputs/dr_benchmark/). Flag `PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER` read at call time (never import — I-cap-005).

1. **ALTITUDE (pivotal):** report-level (post-`generate_multi_section_report`, pre/at
   build_native_gate_b_inputs) vs per-section (`_run_section`)? Forensic strongly recommends
   report-level (S1 trials, missing already lives there). Confirm or correct.
2. **Two-phase** (pre-gen MAPPED-driven retrieval + post-verify VERIFIED ledger) vs a single
   pre-reduce ledger (internally contradictory)? Confirm two-phase.
3. **Extended (non-S0) pre-gen retrieval budget:** reuse #1190 caps as-is, or a separate reserved
   budget under the no-overshoot 1000-fetch envelope (#1168)? Which?
4. **SCOPE (risk-critical):** ship #1213 as INCLUSION + DISCLOSURE ONLY (no 2nd LLM generation —
   lower risk, smaller diff, still "disclose not hallucinate"), deferring the post-verify
   gap-RECOVERY round (re-generate + re-verify recovered slots) to a gated follow-up? Forensic
   recommends the split. Rule.

   manifest-only? (Blind operator + §-1.1 argue for an explicit body section.)
6. **"Required" sourcing:** confirm the ledger inherits the native-scope-template-only contamination
   lock (never the gold rubric).
7. **url_pattern-only entities** (structurally un-flippable by alt-URL retrieval): need a distinct
   "content present but cited URL not canonical" gap note?

# I-perm-021 (#1213) — RequiredEntityLedger: forensic plan (durable)

Forensic by the parallel Claude agent (2026-06-11), persisted so it is not lost.

## TL;DR
The completeness gap is mostly UPSTREAM (retrieval + extraction), NOT reduce-time. POLARIS
already MEASURES required-entity coverage (post-verify, report-level, in `native_gate_b_inputs.py`)
and already has a bounded gap-driven retrieval lane (`run_required_entity_lane`, #1190) — but that
lane is S0-safety-only, fires once pre-generation, and never closes the loop. The honest scope of
#1213 is wiring existing pieces into a closed feedback loop: extend gap-driven retrieval beyond S0
to all required slots, add a POST-strict_verify ledger that forces inclusion of verified slots and
discloses the rest as explicit gaps. It is NOT a reduce-time invention; the owner's proposed
`_run_section` hook is at the WRONG altitude (misses the S1 trial entities). Codex design-gate must
settle the altitude question before any code.

## 1. Root cause (grounded)
- Required entities declared in `config/scope_templates/{clinical,policy,workforce}.yaml`
  `per_query_report_contract[<slug>].required_entities` (id/type/severity/anchor/canonical-id
  doi|pmid|url_pattern/rendering_slot/required_fields; S0 adds s0_category + content reqs).
  e.g. clinical.yaml:283-331 (S1 SURPASS trials, rendering_slot efficacy_surpass_*), 437-525 (S0 safety).
  Loader `native_gate_b_inputs.load_required_entities` (:216-234, fail-closed).
- Coverage MEASURED post-report at Gate-B: `build_native_gate_b_inputs` (:649-783) iterates
  `multi.sections[*].kept_sentences_pre_resolve` (strict_verify-VERIFIED; guard :683-684), computes
  `covered_element_ids` via `_claim_covers_entity` (:514-535) = claim cites evidence whose CANONICAL id
  EXACTLY equals the entity's (`_entity_canonical_match` :293-309). Emits `CoverageLedger` (:778).
  So `missing = required − ⋃covered` is ALREADY computable here — what's missing is the feedback loop.
- REDUCE: two paths. (a) V30 contract path `run_contract_section` (contract_section_runner.py:483,
  dispatched multi_section_generator.py:5112-5128) renders the S1 trial entities by rendering_slot AND
  ALREADY discloses unfillable slots faithfully (compose_gap_payload :370-372; the "did not survive
  strict verification… curator-actionable gap" sentence :993-1019). (b) legacy/distill path `_run_section`
  (:2320), distill REDUCE branch (:1706-1769), then UNCHANGED strict_verify (:2426-2430); zero-verified →
  _GAP_STUB_SENTENCE (:2597-2599) / _NO_EVIDENCE_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE (:2356-2373).
  NEITHER path knows about required_entities. `generate_multi_section_report` (:4711-4812) receives NO
  template/slug/required_entities. So a `_run_section`-local ledger is structurally blind to the S1 trial
  entities AND only fires when PG_SECTION_DISTILL is also on.
- VERIFIED authority = strict_verify on the section OUTPUT (:2430) → kept_sentences_pre_resolve.
- Existing #1190 lane `run_required_entity_lane` (required_entity_retrieval.py:295-428) wired at
  run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4808 (PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_RETRIEVAL, default OFF). Bounded (max 12 entities,
  3 q/entity, 5 results/q, 3 seed URLs/entity :90-100), via run_live_retrieval(seed_only=True), fetched
  rows carry REAL URLs never keyed to an entity_id (:319 the faithfulness invariant). BUT fires once
  pre-gen, only for unsatisfied FrameRows, query map S0-only (:113-123).

## 2. Design — TWO-PHASE ledger (resolves the sequencing contradiction)
The issue's "ledger BEFORE reduce" + "missing = required − verified_bindings" + "strict_verify is sole
VERIFIED authority" CANNOT all hold (strict_verify runs on reduce OUTPUT). Resolve as two phases:
- Phase A (pre-gen, report-level): seed ledger from required_entities; mark MISSING/RETRIEVED/MAPPED
  from CORPUS PRESENCE (does any row carry the entity's canonical id + a usable span?). Phase-A MISSING
  drives the extended pre-gen targeted retrieval (the #1190 lane, past S0).
- Phase B (post-strict_verify, report-level): missing = required − {entity has a VERIFIED binding} via
  the exact `_claim_covers_entity` over kept_sentences_pre_resolve. Authoritative VERIFIED/INCLUDED +
  GAP_DISCLOSED decision.
NEW `src/polaris_graph/generator/required_entity_ledger.py` (pure, DI for retrieval): RequiredSlotState
∈ {MISSING,RETRIEVED,MAPPED,VERIFIED,INCLUDED,GAP_DISCLOSED}; RequiredSlot + RequiredEntityLedger with
missing()/mark_corpus()/mark_verified()/finalize(). Reuse `_claim_covers_entity`+`_entity_canonical_match`
verbatim (do NOT re-implement matching). Gap disclosures are DETERMINISTIC TEMPLATED strings (no LLM),
following _GAP_STUB_SENTENCE / the contract gap-sentence precedents — a structured evidence_gaps list,
never fabricated prose. Bounded recovery round optional (PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER_GAP_ROUNDS default 0).

## 3. Faithfulness (every failure path)
1. Forcing inclusion of an UNVERIFIED slot → forbidden: INCLUDED reachable ONLY from VERIFIED, set ONLY
   by mark_verified reading strict_verify is_verified=True. Ledger never sets is_verified.
2. Gap retrieval relabeling a row as the entity → prevented (#1190 invariant :319; coverage still requires
   EXACT canonical match; alt URL can't flip a url_pattern entity — recovery gets CONTENT in, never forces coverage).
3. evidence_gaps filled with unsupported claim → forbidden: templated strings, no fabricated citation.
4. Re-generation bypassing strict_verify → forbidden: re-gen re-runs UNCHANGED strict_verify (:2426-2430).
5. D8 coverage fraction inflated by ledger credit → must NOT: ledger VERIFIED/INCLUDED set MUST equal
   `_claim_covers_entity` (no new credit path). New credit = §-1.1-lethal, out of scope.
6. MAPPED (distiller fuzzy) leaking into VERIFIED → forbidden: only post-strict_verify bindings transition.

## 4. Edits + default-OFF flag PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER (read at call time, never import — I-cap-005 lesson)
NEW required_entity_ledger.py; EDIT required_entity_retrieval.py (extend query map past S0, accept ledger
missing()); EDIT run_honest_sweep_r3.py (Phase A near :4808; Phase B after generate :5703 + finalize → report
disclosure + manifest). Flag-OFF byte-identical (module never imported, no disclosure, manifest unchanged).

## 5. SEVEN open design questions for the Codex design-gate
1. ALTITUDE (pivotal): per-section (_run_section, owner's hook) vs REPORT-LEVEL (post-generate, pre/at
   build_native_gate_b_inputs)? Evidence strongly favors report-level (S1 trials render via contract path;
   _run_section dark unless PG_SECTION_DISTILL; missing already lives report-level + post-verify). RECOMMEND report-level.
2. SEQUENCING: accept the two-phase split, or insist on a single pre-reduce ledger (internally contradictory)?
3. Pre-gen retrieval budget for the extended non-S0 lane: reuse #1190 caps, or a reserved budget? (no-overshoot
   1000-fetch envelope #1168 — must be explicit.)
4. Post-verify gap-recovery round IN-SCOPE for #1213, or ship INCLUSION+DISCLOSURE ONLY (no 2nd generation,
   lower risk, smaller diff) and defer recovery to a follow-up? RECOMMEND split.
5. evidence_gaps placement: per-section body (like contract gap sentence) vs consolidated "Coverage gaps"
   section vs manifest-only? §-1.1 + blind operator → explicit body section.
6. "Required" sourcing benchmark vs production: ledger MUST use the native scope template only (the §-1.1
   contamination lock native_gate_b_inputs.py:10-16 — NEVER read outputs/dr_benchmark/). Confirm inheritance.
7. MAPPED for url_pattern-only entities: exact-equality means alt URL can't flip coverage; does that class
   need a distinct "content present but cited URL not canonical" note?

## 6. Honest caveats
- Gap is LARGELY UPSTREAM. A reduce-time ledger cannot manufacture coverage; if POLARIS still loses on
  completeness, the residual lever is retrieval breadth/recall (#1204 source-funnel campaign), not this ledger.
- Risk concentration = the gap-recovery round (Q4). Lowest-risk #1213 = inclusion+disclosure ONLY (no 2nd LLM
  generation); recovery round a gated follow-up. RECOMMEND Codex split.
- url_pattern regulatory entities are structurally un-flippable by alt-URL retrieval — ledger gets content in
  + discloses, cannot raise their measured coverage. Don't promise coverage gains for that class.
- Paid §-1.1 smoke genuinely required (the effect + no-fabrication claim can't be proven offline; flag-OFF
  byte-identical can).
- Out of scope: D8 thresholds, new coverage credit (that's #1212 coverage_binder), strict_verify/4-role/the
  operator-locked _entity_canonical_match exact-equality gate.

.\docs\broad_dr_domain_general_plan.md:59:**GAP F (P0, the money-waster on a broad run that PASSES the gates) — strict_verify drops policy-reasoning sentences.** `provenance_generator.py:957-964` drops any sentence with no provenance token; `:1032-1076` requires every decimal to appear in a cited span; `:813-815,1137-1144` requires ≥2 content-word overlap with a single span; the entailment judge (`:1183-1291`) fails-closed on `NEUTRAL`. Frontier reports are ~50-90% INTEGRATIVE synthesis ("residency is not the same as legal sovereignty"; "demand destruction sabotages supply generation") — sentences that carry no decimal, fuse numbers from two sources, or are NEUTRAL w.r.t. any single span. These get mass-dropped; per-section `<min_kept_fraction` triggers one regen (`multi_section_generator.py:1549`), and if every section keeps zero, `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2840-2851` writes `abort_no_verified_sections`. Even short of abort, the surviving "Verified Findings" core is a handful of single-fact sentences and the actual analyst reasoning is relegated to the UNVERIFIED Analyst Synthesis block — inverting the frontier bar.
.\docs\broad_dr_domain_general_plan.md:61:**The trap, stated plainly:** the gates are domain-general, so a broad run will PASS adequacy and BILL the generator. Then the clinical section blueprint (Gap E) + clinical claim-frame prompt (Gap E) + clinical-tuned strict_verify (Gap F) gut the integrative reasoning that IS the frontier bar. Money spent, sub-par report out.
.\docs\broad_dr_domain_general_plan.md:88:- **Verifiable per-claim sourcing.** strict_verify (provenance token + numeric-match + content-overlap, `provenance_generator.py:957-1291`) is domain-AGNOSTIC and kills exactly Gemini's two worst broad-question habits: unhedged invented specifics (q1 "60,000 GPUs / ALPHA-01 / 3.6M backordered", `compare_gemini_q1.md:7`) and citing manufacturer PR / vendor-optimism aggregates as fact (q3 "$180B by 2030" from Meta/Microsoft vendor pages, `compare_gemini_q3.md:248-252`). POLARIS structurally cannot emit a number that isn't in a cited span.

.\docs\broad_dr_domain_general_plan.md:59:**GAP F (P0, the money-waster on a broad run that PASSES the gates) — strict_verify drops policy-reasoning sentences.** `provenance_generator.py:957-964` drops any sentence with no provenance token; `:1032-1076` requires every decimal to appear in a cited span; `:813-815,1137-1144` requires ≥2 content-word overlap with a single span; the entailment judge (`:1183-1291`) fails-closed on `NEUTRAL`. Frontier reports are ~50-90% INTEGRATIVE synthesis ("residency is not the same as legal sovereignty"; "demand destruction sabotages supply generation") — sentences that carry no decimal, fuse numbers from two sources, or are NEUTRAL w.r.t. any single span. These get mass-dropped; per-section `<min_kept_fraction` triggers one regen (`multi_section_generator.py:1549`), and if every section keeps zero, `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2840-2851` writes `abort_no_verified_sections`. Even short of abort, the surviving "Verified Findings" core is a handful of single-fact sentences and the actual analyst reasoning is relegated to the UNVERIFIED Analyst Synthesis block — inverting the frontier bar.
.\docs\broad_dr_domain_general_plan.md:61:**The trap, stated plainly:** the gates are domain-general, so a broad run will PASS adequacy and BILL the generator. Then the clinical section blueprint (Gap E) + clinical claim-frame prompt (Gap E) + clinical-tuned strict_verify (Gap F) gut the integrative reasoning that IS the frontier bar. Money spent, sub-par report out.

.\docs\full_power_polaris_architecture_2026_05_31.md:49:**Stage 8 — 4-role verification (per-claim terminal verdict).** Per atomized claim: **atomize** (Writer splits a sentence with two numbers into two claim-records sharing one span — finer than shipped sentence-level strict_verify) → **Mirror** 2-pass calibration (re-read claim blind to Writer confidence; flag over-claim, emit `calibration_delta`) → **Sentinel** binary RAG-faithfulness gate (grounded y/n; `UNGROUNDED` is **fail-closed** — cannot proceed regardless of Judge) → **Sentinel** qualitative-negation pass (`(did not|no|never|absent|without)+endpoint` must be asserted in the span) → **Judge** terminal 5-enum **VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE** (may downgrade, may NOT upgrade a Sentinel-UNGROUNDED claim) → **python strict_verify** deterministic floor (evidence-id in pool, span bounds valid, every decimal appears verbatim in the span, ≥2 content-word overlap; any failure → DROP, no model override) → **guarded citation-repair** only for span-locality failures (right source, wrong offset): Writer proposes a narrower span from the SAME source, re-judged against the short span; still failing → fail-closed DROP; repair can never invent a source or widen scope. **Model/tool:** `role_pipeline.py` Mirror/Sentinel/Judge (`corpus_relevance_driven_logic.md §4.5`); `generator/strict_verify` (§9.1.3) + M-45 refetch; `openrouter_client.check_family_segregation` (§9.1.1). **Fixes vs shipped:** wires the built-but-not-default gate into the default CLI/UI/queue path (`:3940`); adds atomization, the 5-enum, the negation pass, and the guarded repair (shipped has none — a correct claim with a slightly-off offset is dropped outright, costing coverage). The guarded repair is the corrected design from `project_complete_solution_codex_corrections_2026_05_30` (whole-doc widening would smuggle ungrounded claims; candidate-generator-only + re-judge short span + fail-closed is the safe form).
.\docs\full_power_polaris_architecture_2026_05_31.md:53:**Stage 10 — Output + audit trail (the category frontier does not contest).** Emits: (O1) a per-sentence VERIFIED/PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE/DROPPED **ledger** with the cited span, the Mirror/Sentinel/Judge signals, and the drop-reason — a first-class artifact, not a debug log; (O2) **fail-closed verdict** — if every section fails strict_verify, `report.md` is a pipeline-verdict artifact `abort_no_verified_sections`; rubber-stamp corpus → `abort_corpus_approval_denied` (§9.3); (O3) a **signed audit bundle** (report + ledger + conflict records + `corpus_funnel` + resolved span text, hashed/signed); (O4) the **Proof-Replay split-view** demo centerpiece (click any claim → highlight the exact cited span side-by-side + 4-role verdict chips + decimal-match); (O5) a **saturation/concision report** so concision is auditable, not arbitrary truncation. **Model/tool:** python aggregator over Stage-8 outputs; §9.3 statuses; signed-manifest infra (§9.1); Proof-Replay UI (tasks #448-454, #461). **Fixes vs shipped:** the pipeline emits artifacts but no user-facing ledger, no single signed replayable bundle, and no replay surface; sentences are dropped silently inside strict_verify.

.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:22:The through-line: **quality is WEIGHT, not COUNT, and the weight must (a) actually drive synthesis, (b) discount non-independent corroboration, and (c) be disclosed per claim.** No deployed system does all three. POLARIS's existing per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` provenance + `strict_verify` is the right substrate; the missing layers are a disclosed credibility prior, an independence/echo-chamber collapse, and a per-claim weight-and-disclose UX.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:55:**Relevance to POLARIS:** OpenAI DR is the closest competitor on citation hygiene and the system to beat on faithfulness. Its "explains why each source was chosen" is prose, not an auditable weight — exactly what POLARIS's disclosed credibility prior + span-bound provenance token out-rigors. The 21% unsupported-citation rate and the 79%→17% degradation curve are the concrete failure modes `strict_verify` (numeric match + content-word span overlap) is built to catch. **Perplexity is the strongest single argument FOR the POLARIS thesis:** most citation-visible product, grounds almost nothing — proof that visible citations without span verification are theater.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:205:**Where POLARIS could LEAD, given per-claim provenance.** POLARIS already has the hardest substrate — per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` tokens + `strict_verify` (numeric match + ≥2 content-word span overlap) + zero-verified-abort. That is the verify-against-span layer the entire faithfulness literature says is missing and the layer a citation chip cannot provide. The lead is to extend that substrate from *provenance disclosure* to **provenance + credibility-weight + independent-origin-count + certainty disclosure per claim** — the union that no competitor offers — and to make the credibility weight (a) domain-conditional, (b) independence-aware, and (c) actually drive composition (not sit beside it as a decoration like Consensus's chips).
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:340:7. .\docs\credibility_adaptive_weighting_and_bothsides_recs_2026_06_08.md:1:# POLARIS Source-Credibility & Multi-Perspective Recommendations Report\n### Driving two revisions to `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md` — LAYER 1 (scoring) and LAYER 6 (composition + UI)\n\n**Author:** Lead analyst (Claude). **Date:** 2026-06-08.\n**Inputs:** three research streams (per-question adaptive credibility weighting; neutral multi-perspective presentation; friendly UI/artifact patterns).\n**Status:** revision driver. Each layer below states what it **REPLACES** in the Codex-APPROVE'd 2026-06-07 plan, then the concrete mechanism, auditability, reliability, and POLARIS wiring.\n\n**Invariants preserved across both revisions** (these are not negotiable and the revisions do not weaken them): the LLM credibility-judge is **advisory only** — `strict_verify`'s six per-sentence checks (`provenance_generator.py:1156`) remain the binding faithfulness gate; every new layer is **flag-gated, default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud on missing inputs; weight is **evidence quality/tier, never headcount**; every displayed aggregate is **one click from the cited `[#ev:id:start-end]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict**; the credibility prior stays **self-computed and verifier-overridable** (no single external rater hardwired); independence-collapse (`synthesis/independence_collapse.py`) still runs before any weighted tally; the contested-topic adjudication **reuses the existing 4-role / voter-arbiter pipeline** (`roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py`), open-weight voters only — no parallel path is built.\n\n---\n\n## 1. LAYER 1 (scoring): adaptive per-question credibility-skill, replacing the fixed domain-rubric selector\n\n### 1.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 2a/2b (L1)** selects one of **three fixed reliability rubrics** (clinical → RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy → design-validity; qualitative → CERQual) keyed off the detected domain. That is still a small lookup table: domain → rubric. The operator constraint is explicit — **replace fixed domain rubrics** with a **generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill the LLM applies by reasoning per question**. Research stream #1's central finding is that the frontier (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) has already moved this way, and that Perplexity's baked-in EEAT/domain-authority scoring is the closest-to-forbidden counter-example (gameable, popularity-biased, ~60% Google-overlap). We copy the reasoned-generic camp.\n\nThe revision: **domain stops being a selector and becomes context that primes one generic skill.** There is exactly one credibility skill; the detected `scope_template` domain is passed in as a hint the skill reasons over ("this is a clinical efficacy question, so weight trial design and RoB heavily"), not a branch that swaps rubrics. This scales to 10,000+ fields with zero per-field config.\n\n### 1.2 The exact mechanism — credibility-skill / guardrail prompt × (reliability × relevance)\n\n**Step A — deterministic signals (POLARIS produces these; unchanged substrate).** `authority_model.score_source_authority` and `tier_classifier` already emit, per source, the field-agnostic primitives: `authority_score [0,1]`, `source_class`, `corroboration_count` (independent-host agreement), `authority_confidence`, `signal_scores` (the A–E sub-scores), plus `junk_class` / `predatory_oa` (`source_class.py:71-89`). Independence-collapse (`independence_collapse.py`) emits `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + copy flag. **None of this is replaced.** These are the inspectable, host-name-free, self-computed inputs (LAW VI: zero host literals in code).\n\n**Step B — the credibility skill consumes those signals and weights them per question.** A new pure prompt module, `authority/credibility_skill.py`, runs a **JudgeRank-style 3-step decomposition** (arXiv 2411.00142) — chosen because each step is inspectable text and is itself the audit template:\n\n1. **Query analysis** — identify what THIS question actually needs (the claim type, what would count as strong evidence for it).\n2. **Source analysis** — for each candidate source, a query-AWARE assessment applying **generic SIFT + lateral-reading + provenance heuristics**, NOT a domain table. The skill's instruction block encodes the domain-agnostic primacy heuristics that are OpenAI's own deep-research pattern: *prefer the primary/official source over an aggregator or SEO blog; prefer the original paper over a survey/secondary summary; corroborate laterally (does independent coverage agree); weigh source incentives/track record; downgrade stale-but-authoritative and retracted/superseded* (the latter wires to the existing `supersession.py` Phase 3 signal).\n3. **Two-axis judgment with rationale** — emit, per source, **RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE** (the EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence two-axis, which POLARIS currently conflates into one tier): reliability = internal validity/method given what the skill read in the signals; relevance = directness to this specific question. The product is the per-question credibility weight. **Crucially, the skill is told its priors are the `authority_model` signals and that it may up- or down-weight them with a stated reason** — it does not invent authority from nothing, and it cannot silently override `authority_confidence=LOW` into a fabricated HIGH (thin OpenAlex/ROR signal stays honestly LOW).\n\n**Output schema (machine-parseable, bound to the schema discipline already in §8.3.9):** per source — `{reliability_score, relevance_score, credibility_weight, rationale_string, signals_cited:[...]}`. The weight is **a visible, revisable rationale string, never a hidden scalar and never a fine-tuned-in behavior** (this is why we reject the CAG fine-tune approach from stream #1: opaque learned weights are exactly the auditability failure POLARIS differentiates against — borrow CAG's *evidence* that credibility-conditioning gives noise robustness, and its three generic dimensions of relevance/timeliness/reliability, but implement them at inference time as a prompt-visible signal).\n\n### 1.3 How it stays AUDITABLE (auditable by construction, not bolted on)\n\nThe judgment IS a reasoning trace, so it is emitted verbatim and **bound to the same provenance substrate POLARIS already has**:\n\n- The per-source `credibility_weight` + `rationale_string` + `signals_cited` attach to the source's `AuthorityResult` as **additive fields** (mirroring how `signal_scores`, `junk_class`, `predatory_oa` are already additive side-outputs on the dataclass), emitted on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n- At claim time, the weight flows into the four Phase-1/Phase-8 disclosure fields on `SentenceVerification` (`credibility_weight`, `independent_origin_count`, `certainty_label`, `span_verdict`), so each verified sentence shows **which source was weighted how, and why**, one click from its `[#ev]` span.\n- Because the skill cites the deterministic `signals_cited` it reasoned from, the rationale is **checkable against the inputs** — a reviewer can confirm "preferred A over B because A is the primary trial-registry entry and B a secondary news summary" against the actual `source_class` values. The rationale is a **claim to verify, not ground truth** (post-hoc rationalization risk per stream #1) — so it is advisory and the binding gate stays `strict_verify`.\n\nThis is the §-1.1-compliant story: no metadata/count/string-presence signal, every weight traceable to a cited span and an inspectable reason.\n\n### 1.4 Reliability and where it fails (and the guardrail)\n\nStream #1 is blunt about the failure modes, and they bound the design: LLMs "treat all retrieved documents as equally valid" unless explicitly steered and over-rely on high-frequency claims (arXiv 2505.17762); AuthorityBench (2603.25092) shows authority perception is **inconsistent across domains and highly prompt-sensitive**, struggles with false authority, and exhibits position/verbosity/self-preference bias. Therefore:\n\n- **The skill is ADVISORY to ranking/selection, never the faithfulness gate.** It changes *which rows are billed/weighted*, not *whether a sentence verifies its span*. `strict_verify`, 4-role/two-family segregation, and the independent `corpus_approval_gate` tier-distribution backstop all still run sequentially.\n- **Bias mitigations baked into the prompt:** score rationale-first then number; randomize candidate order to blunt position bias; run pointwise (per-source) to avoid listwise permutation instability.\n- **Offline validation before trust:** the chosen judge model+prompt is validated on an **AuthorityBench-style harness** (authority-PERCEPTION only, per the §6a no-overstatement correction — not as a volume-vulnerability benchmark) plus the existing vax adversarial fixture, before it is allowed on the shipping path. Calibration (Brier/ECE) on the emitted weights runs in the existing Phase 11 harness.\n- **Capture resistance:** multi-signal, self-computed, verifier-overridable — no NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes verdict is hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk).\n\n### 1.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L1\n\n**HAVE:** deterministic authority signals (`authority_model`, `tier_classifier`), the additive `AuthorityResult`/`SentenceVerification` schema, independence-collapse, `scope_templates` domain detection, the JSON-verdict schema discipline.\n**MISSING:** the generic per-question credibility skill itself, and the second (relevance) axis.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 2a/2b):**\n1. `authority/credibility_skill.py` — the pure 3-step (query→source→two-axis-judgment) prompt module emitting `{reliability, relevance, credibility_weight, rationale, signals_cited}`. Domain is an input *hint*, not a rubric switch. (Replaces the fixed-rubric selector in `credibility_prior.py`.)\n2. Add `relevance_score` (and the rationale fields) to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n3. Wire the skill's weight into the L5 weight-mass tally and the L7 disclosure fields — advisory input, not a gate.\n4. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; OFF = the legacy single-scalar path, byte-for-byte.\n\n---\n\n## 2. LAYER 6 (composition + UI): neutral multi-perspective + transparent evidence-weight artifact, replacing the forewarning/fringe framing\n\n### 2.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 7 (L6)** composes contested claims as "consensus at high weight + minority attributed at low weight **with explicit forewarning**, medical-misinfo **low-weight/fringe**, ABSTAIN over fabricated balance." The operator directive is to **replace the forewarning/fringe framing** with a **neutral multi-perspective presentation where competing world-views are shown as legitimate, each carrying its real (possibly asymmetric) evidence weight, and the user judges** — no bias, no stamps, no forewarning prose.\n\n### 2.2 The hard tension, named and resolved (do not skip this)\n\nThere is a real conflict between two binding constraints, and both sides have a lethal failure mode:\n\n- The operator wants **no fringe/forewarning stamp, all sides legitimate, no bias.**\n- The **clinical-safety invariant #5** (binding, preserved) plus Dixon-Clarke 2013 (PubMed 23193194, measured harm: falsely-balanced vaccine reporting *reduced* perceived safety and vaccination intent), Wikipedia FRINGE, and §-1.1 all say **equal treatment of unequal evidence is itself bias** and is "lethal in clinical." I am not authorized to drop invariant #5.\n\n**The reconciliation — handed to us nearly verbatim by all three streams: replace the LABEL with the BASIS.** Wikipedia NPOV's "neutral *editing*, not neutral *content*"; GRADE's "show the basis, let the user infer"; the no-warning rule from stream #2 is satisfiable **if and only if the criteria behind any weight are displayed**. So:\n\n- The **system** stamps nothing — no "fringe," no "debunked," no forewarning sentence. That is the neutrality.\n- The **evidence weight** carries the (often asymmetric) truth, rendered as a visible **per-perspective evidence-basis panel** the user reads and judges. The asymmetry — *14 RCTs + 3 systematic reviews [High]* vs *2 case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]* — becomes **visible in the artifact** instead of **asserted in a stamp**. The user infers; the system does not editorialize.\n- "Legitimate" means **shown with its real (possibly low) weight and one-click drill-down to span + verdict** — NOT "shown as equally supported." A genuine world-view dispute with no empirical consensus (values, forecasting) shows steelmanned sides at honest parity; an empirical dispute with consensus shows the real weight distribution.\n- The medical-misinfo case is handled **without the banned word**: its panel is simply **near-empty of independent high-tier basis**, and where there is no independent consensus the claim **ABSTAINs** ("contested; no independent consensus" — reusing existing refuse/unresolved behavior). This preserves invariant #5's *substance* (not normalized as an equal side) while dropping the stamp.\n\nThis is neutral AND anti-false-balance AND §-1.1-safe simultaneously — the operator's "let the user judge" satisfied by *showing the basis*, not by *equalizing the boxes*.\n\n### 2.3 The exact composition mechanism\n\nPer question, the composition path first **diagnoses the contestation type** (this IS the adaptive guardrail, copied from Anthropic's even-handedness "lack-of-consensus" discriminator + OpenAI's seek-the-truth-together):\n\n- **empirical-with-consensus** (vaccines, climate) → due-weight: each side shown, weight proportional to verified-evidence quality; the asymmetry is the message, carried by the basis panel.\n- **genuine-uncertainty** (open scientific questions) → separate *supported* from *open*; sides at honest parity.\n- **values dispute** (politics, ethics) → **steelman each legitimate view** (Ideological Turing Test, neutral terminology, no verdict); no evidence-weight asymmetry asserted because there is no due weight to defer to.\n- **forecasting** (AI-and-jobs) → scenario spread, projective not factual.\n\nEach perspective is gathered through its **own retrieval lens** (MODS-style per-perspective retrieval + citation, arXiv 2502.00322 — raw LLMs silently drop minority views; per-perspective retrieval prevents the drop), then composed via the **existing 4-role / voter-arbiter agreement** (cross-lineage, open-weight voters — the bridging/balanced-panel analog) — **not a new adjudication path**. Reranking-for-balance (arXiv 2506.15925) selects the most balanced faithful candidate, because direct just-prompt-balanced generation empirically skews. **The weight attached to each perspective is evidence QUALITY × independent-origin count, never document count** — else we rebuild Ground News's source-count bias inside the summarizer.\n\n### 2.4 The friendly UI artifact — design\n\nBuilt on the **components that already exist** (#744 citation chip + hover source card, #745 per-claim verdict chip, #747 Proof Replay split-view, #757 report page, #758 claim-anchored compare view) — not designed from scratch.\n\n**The artifact: a claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** that opens from any contested sentence (reusing the compare-view host), containing per legitimate perspective:\n\n1. **A neutral position header** — "Position held by [who], with this evidence behind it" — no "fringe"/"warning"/"debunked". A steelmanned one-line statement of the view in neutral terms.\n2. **An evidence-basis sub-panel (GRADE-style, the load-bearing element):** the study types / source tiers / independent-origin count behind that perspective, rendered as **frequencies, not a percentage or a lone dot** — "*8 of n: RCTs + systematic reviews [High certainty]*" vs "*2 of n: case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]*". The certainty label is acceptable **only because its downgrade criteria are shown** (study design / consistency / directness / independence). This makes the asymmetry self-evident without a stamp.\n3. **Scite-style polarity badges** (supporting / contrasting / contextualizing) on each cited source — **neutral hue + icon + text label, never color alone** (colorblind safety; red=contrasting reads as red=wrong and biases toward consensus — explicitly avoided).\n4. **Drill-down:** every badge, every basis count, every panel is **one click from the raw `[#ev]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict** — the Scite "self-correcting" property: a mis-classified badge is exposed by the citing sentence sitting right behind it, and a meter without drill-down would violate the §-1.1 metadata-audit ban.\n\n**Panel sizing defeats false balance structurally, not with a caveat:** panels are **sized/annotated by real verified-evidence weight**, not equal-width — because equal real estate reads as equal credibility (the false-balance trap), and a caveat is empirically insufficient (Effectiviology / false-balance literature). The asymmetry is *in the layout and the basis panel*, not asserted in prose.\n\n**Three named anti-patterns explicitly rejected** (all from the streams, all as negative templates):\n- **Ground News source-count / coverage bar** — measures who-is-saying-this (volume), not evidence weight; §-1.1 bans source counts as quality signals. If a composition strip is shown at all, its axis is **evidence tier / source type**, never ideology or headcount, and it is kept visually separate from the weight panel so "many sources" never reads as "true."\n- **Bare confidence/credibility score (% or traffic-light dot)** — the best-documented HARM pattern: overconfident displays (80% shown / 70% accurate) pushed AI-advice adoption 57%→69.6%, 64% of users mis-judged calibration, and in a 2025 breast-cancer CDSS high confidence *reduced* diagnostic accuracy via overreliance. POLARIS's honest replacement is the per-sentence VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict against a real span — never a glanceable dot.\n- **Symmetric 50/50 equal-box presentation** — manufactures false balance; the Dixon-Clarke measured harm.\n\n### 2.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L6\n\n**HAVE:** the disclosure substrate (per-sentence `[#ev]` tokens, `strict_verify` verdict ladder, `SentenceVerification` additive fields), the existing 4-role/voter-arbiter agreement, independence-collapse for origin-count, and the full UI component kit (citation chip, verdict chip, Proof Replay, compare view).\n**MISSING:** the contestation-type classifier, per-perspective retrieval + composition that defeats false balance by weight, and the Perspectives artifact rendering basis panels.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 7):**\n1. A **contestation-type classifier** (empirical-consensus / genuine-uncertainty / values / forecasting) priming the composition rule — replaces the fixed forewarning+fringe rule.\n2. **Per-perspective retrieval + balance-rerank composition** over the post-collapse origins, weighting each perspective by verified-evidence quality × independent-origin count (not count).\n3. A **steelman + GRADE-style evidence-basis renderer** feeding a new **claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** in the compare-view host: neutral headers, frequency-framed basis sub-panels, Scite-style neutral polarity badges, full span-verdict drill-down, weight-proportional sizing.\n4. Validated with a **paired-prompt neutrality eval** (Anthropic's open-sourced political-neutrality harness, adapted so it does NOT reward equal treatment of unequal evidence) + the vax adversarial fixture (asserts the asymmetry renders, no stamp, no 50/50).\n5. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; ABSTAIN reuses existing refuse/unresolved behavior; forewarning prose removed, replaced by the basis panel.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Consolidated "build" summary\n\n| Layer | Replaces (2026-06-07 plan) | POLARIS builds | Binding gate untouched |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **L1 scoring** | Fixed 3-rubric domain selector (Phase 2a/2b) | `credibility_skill.py` — generic 3-step SIFT/lateral-reading skill, reliability×relevance, rationale bound to `AuthorityResult`/`[#ev]`; domain as hint not switch; `relevance_score` axis | `strict_verify` binding; skill advisory; corpus_approval backstop |\n| **L6 composition+UI** | Forewarning + fringe framing (Phase 7) | Contestation-type classifier + per-perspective retrieval/balance-rerank weighted by quality×origin-count + steelman/GRADE-basis "Perspectives" panel (replace label with basis) | 4-role/voter-arbiter reused (no parallel path); ABSTAIN reuses refuse/unresolved; invariant #5 substance preserved |\n\nHonest residual: L1's skill is prompt-sensitive and must clear offline AuthorityBench-style + calibration validation before it is trusted on the shipping path; L6's steelman is the hardest element to audit mechanically (no test certifies an argument is the strongest version vs a disguised strawman — the paired-prompt eval is the best available check, itself a judgment). Both remain advisory; `strict_verify` carries faithfulness.\n\n---\n\n## 4. .\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:9:- **LEGACY** (`multi_section_generator.py:1754-2057`): the writer emits only SHORT `[ev_XXX]` markers and never picks character offsets. `_rewrite_draft_with_spans` (`live_deepseek_generator.py:335-386`) recognizes them via `_EV_MARKER_RE` (`:64` = `\[([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\]`) and calls `_find_best_span_for_sentence` (`:244-332`), which scans the whole `direct_quote` and returns the span maximizing decimal + content-word overlap with the ACTUAL sentence — i.e. it computes spans **designed to pass strict_verify**.

Even in-bounds-but-off offsets miss the ≥2 content-word overlap (`:1809-1813`) + enforce-mode entailment (`strict_verify.py:173,187`). A MAP support_quote span is NARROW (one atomic finding); the REDUCE sentence is a SYNTHESIS, so a narrow span shares <2 content words with it.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:14:- **Killer B:** `filter_and_strip_reduce_markers` hard-required a KNOWN `[[finding:fXXX]]` marker whose synthetic id the model must reproduce verbatim. If the model didn't echo it, the sentence died at the filter before strict_verify ran.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:16:**Refuted suspects:** (d) "different text" — strict_verify resolves against `direct_quote`, the SAME text the distiller validates against (refuted); (e) reasoning-starvation — the REDUCE wrote 50/57/709 content tokens, yet 0 survived (refuted). The `PG_DISTILL_DEBUG` dump confirmed: ledger=1 finding, REDUCE raw prose present, but the model emitted `[#ev:f004_000:5430-5539]` (finding_id as evidence_id, on a separate sentence) → filter kept only the bare token, prose dropped.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:19:All three acted MAP/ledger-side: (1) span-recovery improved the LEDGER not the model-typed OUTPUT offsets; (2) the relaxed marker filter still kept the `has_known_finding` gate; (3) non-blocking per-finding entailment grew the ledger but the final per-sentence strict_verify on the model-authored token was unchanged. The wall is the REDUCE-output citation FORMAT, which none touched. (This empirically refuted the earlier H1 "entailment starves the ledger" call — fix #3 changed nothing.)
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:21:## THE FIX (both agree; strict_verify UNWEAKENED)
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:27:**Faithfulness untouched:** `strict_verify` still re-validates every span (numbers-in-span + ≥2 overlap + enforce entailment). **Map-reduce is NOT incompatible with strict_verify — only emitting final machine tokens FROM the LLM is.** This is also exactly what the distiller's own module docstring already described ("cite findings with the same legacy `[ev_XXX]` markers"); the implementation had drifted from its stated contract.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:44:**Recall root cause (both):** `_validate_finding` step 4 `_all_numbers_in_span` (`evidence_distiller.py:584`) is a HARD reject when a claim's declared number is not inside the model's NARROW `support_quote`. But that quote never reaches the output — the REDUCE writes fresh prose cited `[ev_XXX]`, and the unchanged legacy `_rewrite_draft_with_spans` → `_find_best_span_for_sentence` (`live_deepseek_generator.py:244`) re-fits an 800-char prose-matched span over the WHOLE ~24.6k-char `direct_quote`, then strict_verify (`require_number_match=True`) re-checks numbers against THAT span. So step 4 is STRICTER than the final gate and pure recall loss with zero faithfulness benefit. Concrete killer (Codex): the CDC stat "14 (95% CI 4–44)" tokenizes "4–44" as ONE range token here while the model declares "4" and "44" separately → a perfectly extractable numeric finding is rejected before REDUCE; final strict_verify normalizes the range dash and would accept/drop the published span correctly. Evidence step-4-dominant: legacy's 6 verified are all numeric-heavy (OR 14, CI 4–44, 22%/10, 37%/17, 5,876 genomes); the lone distill survivor is the ONLY non-numeric claim — exactly what a narrow numbers-in-span filter leaves standing.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:48:**Faithfulness untouched:** `strict_verify` on the final REDUCE prose stays the SOLE publication authority (numbers-in-span AND ≥2 content-word overlap AND enforce-mode entailment); 4-role / D8 byte-untouched. A genuinely fabricated number cannot reach the report — the final gate drops it.
.\docs\keystone_collapse_forensic_consolidated.md:52:**Applied:** `evidence_distiller.py:583-593` step-4 made non-blocking (+ debug log); `test_map_rejects_out_of_span_numbers` → `test_map_keeps_out_of_span_numbers_nonblocking_1217` (asserts KEPT at MAP, final strict_verify is the number authority). 17/17 distiller tests pass. .\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:65:- **Regime C — modeled-vs-sourced (gap 15, NEW):** computed numbers carry a `[#calc:<model_id>:<field>]` (a.k.a. `[#model:…←ev_ids:modeled=vars]`) token; verified against the **computation record**: output matches the executed script verbatim; every *input* is itself Regime-A-grounded; formula id is in an audited registry; the "(modeled assumption)" label is *enforced* (hard drop if missing). Routing signal, not just a display label — this prevents strict_verify from gutting gap 9's own output.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:95:**Critical finding (re-verified this session):** the substrate is ~70% built and DISABLED on the production path. `tools/code_executor.py` is a full **PAL** runtime (LLM writes Python; AST-validated import allowlist numpy/pandas/scipy/math; reflection blocklist; socket-kill preamble; restricted-env subprocess; 30s timeout; retry-on-error at `generate_and_execute_analysis()`). `tools/tool_registry.py` has `execute_python`/`comparison_table`/`meta_analysis`, each returning `source_evidence_ids` with per-item `[CITE:ev_xxx]`. `tools/react_agent.py` orchestrates them. But `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` **never imports** `react_agent`/`build_default_registry`/`code_executor` — it calls `generate_multi_section_report` + the unverified `analyst_synthesis` only. So gap 9 is a **re-wire + provenance-binding + faithfulness-wedge** problem, not greenfield. (PAL: Gao et al. ICML 2023 https://arxiv.org/pdf/2211.10435 , https://github.com/reasoning-machines/pal ; on-prem break-even template https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.18101 ; FinSphere tool-augmented analyst https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.12399 )

.\outputs\codex_findings\round_1\claude_response.md:57:  `generate_multi_section_report(...)` is called

.\outputs\codex_findings\round_1\claude_response.md:95:strict_verify ..." — no reader can confuse it with a content report.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v29_true_root_cause\findings.md:3:The code path supports that reading. The outline planner explicitly says "if the evidence doesn't support a topic, don't include it." The section generator writes from an evidence subset, not from a required editorial frame. `strict_verify` then acts only on what was written. So the real failure is: no frame, no slot obligation, no bind between slot and source, and verification that is purely retrospective. In that design, primaries will always be fragile. Retrieval misses them, selection can displace them, and even when injected, the model can ignore them by writing around them. V29 proved that.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v29_true_root_cause\findings.md:14:On `strict_verify`: it is not the root cause, and I would not relax it. `strict_verify` is doing what it should do: refusing to bless claims that are not grounded in retrieved text. The problem is upstream. Today, if a frame element is not retrievable in machine-readable form, the element silently disappears because nothing in the pipeline reserves an empty slot and emits an explicit coverage failure. In a frame-first design, `strict_verify` remains strict, but the output contract changes from omission to explicit insufficiency: "SURPASS-2 primary publication not retrievable from accessible sources; subsection limited to metadata-level reporting." That is honest and structurally stable. The current architecture converts retrieval failure into content absence. That is the real bug.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v29_true_root_cause\findings.md:26:3. Keep `strict_verify` intact.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v29_fix_plan_review_pass1\findings.md:23:5. **M-52 mutation contract**: when adding a live-corpus row to `evidence_pool`, require the same fields strict_verify and bibliography rendering need: `evidence_id`, `direct_quote`, `source_url`, `title`, `tier`, and any citation metadata currently consumed downstream.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v29_fix_plan_review_pass1\findings.md:49:3. M-53 custody telemetry third, computed after strict_verify and bibliography numbering.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v28_strategic_path\findings.md:11:The strict_verify gate is both the reason POLARIS is credible and the reason cheap imitation of competitor prose fails. Competitors can quote or synthesize trial numbers from memory or inaccessible primary papers. POLARIS must match claims to fetched direct_quote text. For paywalled NEJM/Lancet primaries with thin fetched text, strict_verify blocks the exact numeric specificity that wins clinical dimensions. The answer is not to discard strict_verify globally. The answer is to make primary publication reachability and quote adequacy a first-class pipeline contract before generation begins.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v28_strategic_path\findings.md:23:I would not choose gamma as the main path. Relaxing strict_verify for narrative sections may help Gemini-style mechanism prose, but it attacks the wrong bottleneck and risks losing POLARIS's strongest brand: traceable claims. A narrower version is acceptable later: strict numeric verification for all numeric claims, with a separate "synthesis-only" mode for nonnumeric interpretation that still requires same-paragraph evidence support. That is a refinement, not the core roadmap.
.\outputs\codex_findings\v28_strategic_path\findings.md:37:V31 should address mechanism and narrative: primary clamp/PK extraction first, then review-based synthesis second, with numeric strict_verify retained and nonnumeric synthesis clearly evidence-bounded. Expected outcome: Narrative depth can compete with Gemini without fabricating. Risk: medium because paywalled mechanism papers may still have thin text.

.\outputs\audits\I-bug-092\claude_audit.md:14:The architectural diagnosis: strict_verify enforces **provenance presence** (token format + lexical overlap), not **provenance correctness** (semantic entailment between sentence and span). The current design assumes lexical overlap implies content fidelity. Empirically with capable generators, this assumption breaks — they generate plausible-sounding extensions of the source's topic that share words but introduce unsourced facts.

See manifest.frame_coverage_report and human_gap_tasks.json for per-entity detail.[2]\n\n### Prebiotic / dietary-fiber intake and colorectal-cancer risk\n\nExposure: dietary fibre and whole grains.[1] Population: more than 1.7 million participants.[1] Outcome: colorectal cancer.[1] Dose response: 0.90 (0.86 to 0.94) for each 10 g/day intake.[1] The pooled analysis included more than 1.7 million participants.[1] In the dose-response analysis, each 10 g/day increment in intake was associated with a relative risk of 0.90.[1] The 95% confidence interval for this estimate ranged from 0.86 to 0.94.[1] Colorectal cancer incidence was the outcome of interest in all included cohorts.[1] The more than 1.7 million participants contributed to the statistical power of the analysis.[1]\n\n### Pathogenic bacteria and their genotoxic metabolites in colorectal carcinogenesis\n\nPathogen: Escherichia coli.[3] Mechanism: alkylate DNA on adenine residues4,5 and induces double-strand breaks.[3] Mutational signature: distinct mutational signature.[3] Toxic metabolite or virulence factor: colibactin.[3] This bacterium produces the genotoxic metabolite colibactin.[3] Colibactin is a virulence factor that damages host DNA.[3] The mechanism of colibactin's genotoxicity involves DNA alkylation.[3] Specifically, colibactin alkylates DNA on adenine residues4,5.[3] Following alkylation, colibactin induces double-strand breaks.[3] The induction of double-strand breaks is a direct outcome of colibactin's activity.[3] As a result of this DNA damage, a distinct mutational signature is generated.[3] This mutational signature is characteristic of colibactin exposure.[3] The distinct mutational signature can be detected in colorectal carcinogenesis.[3] Colibactin is a genotoxin that targets DNA.[3] The genotoxic metabolite colibactin is directly responsible for the DNA damage.[3] Ultimately, the mechanism of colibactin involves alkylating DNA on adenine residues4,5 and inducing double-strand breaks, leaving a distinct mutational signature.[3]\n\n### Probiotic contraindications and at-risk populations\n\nContract-bound content for probiotic_immunocompromised_contraindication did not survive strict verification against retrieved primary source text; this slot is a curator-actionable gap. See manifest.frame_coverage_report and human_gap_tasks.json for per-entity detail.[4]\n\n### Safety\n\nAmong 125 patients with nonblood *Saccharomyces* culture findings, 24 (19%) had used the probiotic, versus 2% (3/123) of controls (OR 10, 95% CI 3–32).[4] Consequently, *S. cerevisiae* var. *boulardii* probiotics are not recommended for patients with indwelling catheters, those who are immunocompromised, or the critically ill.[4] In one trial, a multispecies probiotic preparation administered to patients with severe acute pancreatitis was associated with increased mortality. *Escherichia coli* carrying the pks pathogenicity island produces colibactin, a genotoxin that alkylates DNA on adenine residues and induces double-strand breaks.[4].[3] Cyclomodulin-producing *E. coli* were present in biopsies from 70% of colorectal cancer patients compared with only 3% of healthy controls.[5] Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) from Gram-negative bacteria such as *Fusobacterium* and *Bacteroides* activates NF-κB signaling, driving inflammation and colorectal cancer metastasis.[6].[7] A retrospective registry study across five Finnish university hospitals from 2009 to 2018 identified 46 patients with *Saccharomyces* fungemia; at least 20 (43%) were using *Saccharomyces boulardii* probiotic, and compared with a control group with bacteremia or candidemia, the odds ratio for use of an *S. boulardii* probiotic was 14 (95% CI 4–44).[4] Human intestinal organoids were exposed to genotoxic pks+ *E. coli* by repeated luminal injection over five months; whole-genome sequencing of clonal organoids before and after this exposure revealed a distinct mutational signature that was absent from organoids injected with isogenic pks-mutant bacteria.[3] The same mutational signature was detected in a subset of 5,876 human cancer genomes from two independent cohorts, predominantly in colorectal cancer.[3] Butyrate, a short-chain fatty acid, stimulates proliferation at low concentrations but suppresses it at high concentrations.[6]\n\n### Dose Response\n\nFor total dietary fiber, each 10 g/day increment was associated with a summary relative risk (RR) of 0.90 (95% CI 0.86 to 0.94) across 16 studies including 14,514 cases among 1,985,552 participants, with no significant heterogeneity (I²=0%).[1] In contrast, fruit fiber intake was not significantly associated with CRC risk (RR 0.93, 95% CI 0.82 to 1.05 per 10 g/day), and vegetable fiber showed no association (RR 0.98, 95% CI 0.91 to 1.06 per 10 g/day).[1] Similarly, a review of harnessing gut microbiota for CRC therapy highlights the potential of microbiome modulators but emphasizes the need for rigorous validation and biomarker development before clinical translation.[8] A systematic review and meta-analysis of 25 prospective studies examined the association between dietary fiber and whole grain intake and colorectal cancer (CRC) risk[1] Cereal fiber demonstrated a similar protective dose-response: each 10 g/day increase yielded an RR of 0.90 (0.83 to 0.97) based on 8 studies with 9,487 cases among 1,471,756 participants.[1] Whole grain intake also exhibited a significant inverse dose-response: an increment of three servings daily (90 g/day) was associated with an RR of 0.83 (0.78 to 0.89) across 6 studies with 7,941 cases among 774,806 participants.[1]\n\n### Long-term Outcomes\n\nA meta-analysis across solid tumors indicated that combining FMT with immune checkpoint inhibitors improved pooled objective response rates to 43.1% compared to 36.9% with immunotherapy alone.[9] Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) has shown potential to augment therapies for colorectal cancer.[9] In a study, patients with refractory, MSS metastatic CRC receiving tislelizumab and fruquintinib achieved a median progression-free survival of 9.6 months and median overall survival of 13.7 months, with an objective response rate of 20% and a disease control rate of 95%.[9] Probiotic modulation with CBM588 (Clostridium butyricum) combined with cabozantinib and nivolumab significantly increased response rates (74% vs 20%, P=0.01) in a trial of metastatic renal cell carcinoma.[9]\n\n### Limitations\n\nLimitations: The evidence base is heavily skewed, with only 17% of sources classified as T1 primary studies and 62% falling into an unknown tier, which undermines the reliability of the evidence hierarchy. Three high-severity contradictions were detected: sources disagree sharply on the effect on ttr (relative difference 12275.0%), on ttr at 0.5 mg (25.0% difference), and on weight loss (212.5% difference), indicating substantial inconsistency in key outcomes. The evidence horizon spans from 2010-01-01 to the present, potentially omitting earlier foundational research and limiting the temporal scope of the analysis.\n\n## Methods\nPre-registered protocol.json (SHA-256 feb9fd7172651bfc...).\nCorpus: Serper + Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex live retrieval, augmented by domain backends (clinical: domain_backends(clinical): {'europe_pmc': 21}).\nRetrieval fetch outcome: 375 of 740 candidate sources fetched; 365 failed or timed out.\nGenerator model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (multi-section: outline + 6 parallel sections + strict_verify + regen-on-failure).\nEvaluator model: google/gemma-4-31b-it (different family).\nSources classified using T1-T7 tier taxonomy.\nInclusion / exclusion per clinical template. bold; citations are the body's._\n\n- **Efficacy.** Population: patients with advanced Parkinson's disease and severe motor symptoms.[1]\n- **Mechanism.** Complication type: hardware complications.[2]\n- **Long-term Outcomes.** The Movement Disorder Society-sponsored revision of the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS), validated in a study of 877 patients from 39 sites, demonstrated high internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha = 0.79–0.93 across parts) and strong correlation with the original UPDRS (ρ = 0.96), providing a comprehensive framework for tracking these changes across four parts: non-motor experiences of daily living, motor experiences of daily living, motor examination, and motor complications.[3]\n- **Safety.** The FDA labeling for carbidopa-levodopa explicitly warns that patients should be observed carefully when the dosage is reduced abruptly or discontinued, especially if they are receiving neuroleptics.[6]\n- **Population Subgroups.** The choice between rechargeable and non‑rechargeable IPGs was significantly associated with affordability (χ²=19.13, p<0.001), and remote programming capability influenced brand selection.[10]\n\n### Efficacy\n\nPopulation: patients with advanced Parkinson's disease and severe motor symptoms.[1] Intervention: this treatment plus medication.[1] Comparator: medication alone.[1] Primary endpoint: the changes from baseline to six months in the quality of life, as assessed by the Parkinson's Disease Questionnaire (PDQ-39), and the severity of symptoms without medication, according to the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale, part III (UPDRS-III).[1] Timepoint: six months.[1] Study design: randomized-pairs trial.[1]\n\n### Mechanism\n\nComplication type: hardware complications.[2] The Movement Disorder Society-sponsored Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale, known as the MDS-UPDRS, structures the clinical assessment of Parkinson's disease into four distinct and complementary parts.[3] Whereas Part I addresses the non-motor domain, Part II shifts the focus to motor aspects of daily function.[3] However, Part IV specifically records motor complications, distinguishing them from the core motor examination.[3] The combined clinimetric results of this study support the validity of the MDS-UPDRS for rating PD.[3] The primary source identifies hardware complications as a distinct category of DBS-related adverse events.[2] In contrast, the source does not offer guidance on daily life adjustments or support strategies for patients experiencing hardware complications.[2]\n\n### DBS device contraindications and dopaminergic-withdrawal dosing safety\n\nContract-bound content for dbs_device_mri_safety did not survive strict verification against retrieved primary source text; this slot is a curator-actionable gap. See manifest.frame_coverage_report and human_gap_tasks.json for per-entity detail.[4]\n\n### Long-term Outcomes\n\nThe Movement Disorder Society-sponsored revision of the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS), validated in a study of 877 patients from 39 sites, demonstrated high internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha = 0.79–0.93 across parts) and strong correlation with the original UPDRS (ρ = 0.96), providing a comprehensive framework for tracking these changes across four parts: non-motor experiences of daily living, motor experiences of daily living, motor examination, and motor complications.[3] In advanced stages, symptoms are severe and daily life usually requires full-time support.[5]\n\n### Safety\n\nThe FDA labeling for carbidopa-levodopa explicitly warns that patients should be observed carefully when the dosage is reduced abruptly or discontinued, especially if they are receiving neuroleptics.[6] DBS hardware complications are relatively common; in a prospective series of 81 consecutive patients with PD undergoing subthalamic nucleus (STN) DBS followed for a mean of 17 months, hardware complications occurred in 26.2% of patients (atom_009).[2] Specific complications included infections requiring system removal in 2.5% (atom_010) and implantable pulse generator (IPG) removal in 3.7% (atom_011), misplaced leads in 12.5% (atom_012), and lead migration, fracture, or IPG malfunction.[2] A review of the FDA MAUDE database over 10 years identified 221 unique adverse events, with infection being the most common (16.2%) (atom_013) and lead migration accounting for 8.6% (atom_014).[7] Over a third (40%) of all devices with adverse events required return to the operating room for explant or revision (atom_003, atom_004).[7] A review of postoperative psychosis after DBS reported a lifetime incidence of psychosis of 28.3% (atom_018) in PD patients following DBS, underscoring the need for vigilance.[8] Additionally, implantable pulse generator expiration can precipitate a movement disorder emergency with severe parkinsonian worsening or akinetic crisis, necessitating urgent medical evaluation.[9] Abrupt reduction or discontinuation of carbidopa-levodopa has been associated with a symptom complex resembling neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS), characterized by hyperthermia, muscle rigidity, altered consciousness, and autonomic dysfunction.[6] Psychosis can emerge after STN DBS, as illustrated by clinical cases where patients developed paranoid ideation, hallucinations, and delusions within months of surgery, requiring antipsychotic treatment.[8] Patients taking dopaminergic medications may experience intense urges to gamble, increased sexual urges, binge eating, and other compulsive behaviors, which may resolve with dose reduction or discontinuation[6]\n\n### Population Subgroups\n\nThe choice between rechargeable and non‑rechargeable IPGs was significantly associated with affordability (χ²=19.13, p<0.001), and remote programming capability influenced brand selection.[10] Despite this, a preliminary survey of 17 DBS patients (85% of those approached) found that 76.5% used their patient programmer once per month or less, 17.7% never used it, and the most common function was checking battery life (58.8%).[11] Adaptive DBS shows promise, as one study reported that overall well‑being significantly improved with adaptive stimulation (p=0.007).[12] Caregivers of intermediate PD spent 6.6 hours per day caring, compared to 3.2 hours for early PD, and were less likely to be employed (25.3% vs 42.4%).[13] Personalized DBS programming using machine learning to optimize gait has produced meaningful improvements in walking, addressing a common post‑DBS challenge.[14] Remote patient monitoring devices led 79.9% of participants to agree that they improved mobility, and 48% perceived themselves as safer.[15] However, a meta‑synthesis of wearable device experiences identified barriers including discomfort, emotional distress, and interface difficulties that hinder long‑term adherence.[16] 87.3% of DBS patients used rechargeable IPGs (r-IPGs), 87.7% were satisfied with their device; among those with r-IPGs, 40.6% felt confident handling the device within one week of discharge, 71.4% recharged independently, and the mean recharge interval was 4.3 days.[10] Caregivers of people with advanced PD had significantly higher perceived burden (ZBI 34.5 vs 24.7 for early PD) and were more likely to take medication due to caregiving (34.7% vs 13.8%).[13] Assistive technologies also contribute: a scoping review reported that home automation significantly increased quality of life (p<0.001), and 63.5% of participants used speech‑to‑text functions to cope with tremors.[15]\n\n### Limitations\n\nLimitations: The pipeline's tier distribution reveals a heavy reliance on lower-quality evidence, with only 10% of sources classified as T1 primary studies and a striking 70% falling into the UNKNOWN tier. Six high-severity contradictions were detected, including a 972.4% relative difference in mortality estimates and a 481.0% divergence in hazard ratio, indicating sources disagree sharply on both magnitude and direction of key outcomes. The evidence horizon spans from 2010-01-01 to the present, but the predominance of unclassified sources and sparse representation in tiers T2 (1%), T3 (1%), and T5 (0%) further limit confidence in the synthesized findings.\n\n## Methods\nPre-registered protocol.json (SHA-256 36a59dc009aa949f...).\nCorpus: Serper + Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex live retrieval, augmented by domain backends (clinical: domain_backends(clinical): {'europe_pmc': 27}).\nRetrieval fetch outcome: 478 of 740 candidate sources fetched; 262 failed or timed out.\nGenerator model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (multi-section: outline + 6 parallel sections + strict_verify + regen-on-failure).\nEvaluator model: google/gemma-4-31b-it (different family).\nSources classified using T1-T7 tier taxonomy.\nInclusion / exclusion per clinical template.
