src\polaris_graph\generator\multi_section_generator.py:1325:13. **Policy-scope disambiguation (M-NEW-1, GH#422)**: When a paragraph names a specific program (Bill C-64, ACA, MACRA, EU AI Act, Article 34.7 CUSMA review, a particular budget line, etc.) and the evidence pool also contains projections / cost estimates / impact analyses for a RELATED-BUT-BROADER scope (universal single-payer projection vs phase-1 narrow program; comprehensive coverage estimate vs narrow amendment; multi-jurisdiction equivalent of a single-state rule), do NOT silently fold the broader projection into the narrow-program paragraph. When citing numbers from the broader scope, EXPLICITLY label the scope-attribution INLINE in the SAME sentence as the citation. Required pattern: write "PBO 2023 universal single-payer projection estimates the additional cost at $11.2B in 2024-25 [ev_X]" — NOT "the incremental cost is $11.2B in 2024-25 [ev_X]" inside a paragraph that opens with Bill C-64 phase-1 (which covers only contraception and diabetes medications). The decimal and the citation are correct; the missing scope label is what makes the conflation. Same evidence-ID, additional 4-8 word scope phrase before the citation. This rule fires regardless of which section the named-program paragraph appears in (Regulatory, Comparative, Economic, etc.). Failure mode this rule prevents: a reader concludes a narrow program will cost the broader program's projected figure. Concrete examples (placeholders only): GOOD: "Bill C-64 covers a defined set of contraception and diabetes medications [ev_A]. The PBO's 2023 cost estimate of a universal single-payer drug plan modeled on an expanded Quebec formulary projects incremental public-sector cost of $11.2B in 2024-25 [ev_B]." — distinct scopes named, decimals attributed to broader scope. BAD: "Under Bill C-64, the incremental cost to the public sector is estimated at $11.2 billion in 2024-25 [ev_B]." — cites the PBO universal-plan source as if it were a Bill C-64 phase-1 projection.

docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:171:**Cons.** **License is the dealbreaker for Carney**: Llama Community License has (a) a 700M MAU clause that converts the licence to "Meta's sole discretion" beyond that scale [^llama_lic], (b) a competitor restriction blocking entire industries, (c) a ban on using Llama to train other models — which restricts the CHECK-style ensemble distillation pattern POLARIS uses. Even for Carney delivery (one-shot gift), the trained-other-model clause may bind if POLARIS uses any Llama-derived distillation. **Sovereignty concern**: Meta is US-origin. While the WEIGHTS can be self-hosted in Canada, the license is Meta-controlled and the policy is US-export-regulated for some destinations. MMLU-Pro 68.9 — weakest reasoning of the frontier candidates. GPQA Diamond 50.5.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:181:**Cons.** Vectara HHEM-2.3 = **9.5% halluc rate, 94.5% answer rate** — second-worst of the candidates (only Qwen3-235B-A22B at 9.3% is comparable). MMLU-Pro UNKNOWN for 4.6 explicitly (4.5 = 84.6). Less stable maintenance cadence than DeepSeek / Qwen.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:197:### 3.9 Verifier candidates (compressed narratives)
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:221:Apache 2.0 (Qwen3, Mistral Large 3) and MIT (DeepSeek V4 Pro, V3.2-Exp, GLM-4.6) are tied for cleanest commercial usability. Llama Community (Llama 3.3 70B) is the weakest license among capable candidates — the 700M MAU clause is forward-looking risk but the "ban on training other models" clause is immediate risk for any ensemble distillation. docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:227:**Genuinely missing across the entire candidate set**:

docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:242:**The gap that matters most for Carney delivery**: the citation-existence + source-attribution-honesty dimension. POLARIS's mission requires every claim to cite a real source that actually supports the claim. ScholarQABench, CiteME, GhostCite, CiteAudit all exist as benchmarks but none of them have published scores for the candidate set above. **This must be measured in-house** (§6.1).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:272:**Acknowledged weakness:** 8.6% Vectara HHEM-2.3 hallucination rate is the highest of the candidate set. **This is mitigated by the layered verifier architecture** (§5.2), not by switching the generator. Per `feedback_top_tier_model_only_2026_05_25` ("stop loving the old LLM model, debug latest model bugs, don't revert"), the answer to V4 Pro's hallucination delta is NOT to fall back to V3.2-Exp; the answer is to harden the downstream verification layer.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:276:**Tertiary (sovereignty-preferred alternative, NOT replacement):** Mistral Large 3 (4.5% Vectara halluc on the L2-2411 sibling, 99.9% answer rate, Apache 2.0, **EU-origin**). If operator at any future point relaxes the "non-US only" to "EU-preferred for Canadian bilateral", Mistral Large 3 becomes the strongest single-model candidate by the multi-parameter rollup: best license, best sovereignty story for Carney, top-tier hallucination, top-tier multilingual incl FR. The cost is reasoning depth (GPQA Diamond 43.9 vs V4 Pro 90.1) which is the single dimension where Mistral Large 3 substantially lags.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:292:**Verifier candidates explicitly NOT recommended:**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:315:The matrix has 23 MUST-HAVE generator parameters and 9 MUST-HAVE verifier parameters. **The following parameters have NO published cell for the candidate set and require in-house evaluation:**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:319:1. **Citation existence + source attribution honesty (params 14-15)** — the dimension that matters MOST for Carney. ScholarQABench / CiteME / GhostCite all exist; the candidate set does not appear on them. **In-house: fetch 100 generated reports from V4 Pro + Qwen3-8B + Mistral Large 3, sample 10 claims per report, validate DOIs via OpenAlex API, validate paper title exact-match via PubMed/arXiv, validate cited span supports claim via human review.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:321:2. **LongGenBench / LongWriter 2K/4K/8K (param 3)** — POLARIS's reports are 1.5K-3K words. Custom harness: prompt each candidate to produce 2K-word and 4K-word reports on a fixed scientific question, score completion (finish_reason), score length (word count), score quality (PRISMA-aligned rubric).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:323:3. **Calibration (ECE / Brier) (param 19)** — no candidate has published ECE. The CHECK-style ensemble pattern requires per-token log-probabilities + cross-model KL; calibration of those probs vs ground truth is critical. **In-house: log probabilities on 1000 known-answer questions from each candidate, compute ECE.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:327:5. **French (params 8a-b) per-candidate** — MMLU-ProX leaderboard shows aggregate; need FR slice specifically. **In-house: subset MMLU-ProX FR + FrenchBench Pro + COLE, run all candidates.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:329:6. **Self-consistency / SelfCheckGPT (param 18)** — temp-0 N-run semantic variance. POLARIS regenerates failed sections; self-consistency = upper bound on regeneration quality. **In-house: 10 runs per candidate at temp 0.1 on 50 fixed questions, compute pairwise semantic similarity (SentenceBERT cosine).**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:331:7. **Output length completion (param 20)** — at 2K/4K/8K target, what's the truncation rate? **In-house: 100 prompts per length per candidate, finish_reason audit.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:335:1. **Adversarial robustness (Param H)** — none of the verifier candidates have published RAG-injection robustness scores. **In-house: AgentDojo subset evaluation of the verifier as a passive observer (verifier's job is to flag, not act).**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:339:3. **French (Param G)** — VerifAI uses XLM-RoBERTa sibling for multilingual but per-candidate FR-NLI quality unknown. **In-house: XNLI-FR + custom French claim-evidence pairs (assemble from Cochrane FR Cochrane Library + INESSS reports).**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:341:4. **Reasoning trace schema compliance (Param J)** — Codex's revised structured-span schema (label + score + evidence_span + contradiction_span + abstain_reason). **In-house: grammar-constrained vLLM run of each LLM-judge candidate against the schema, parse-rate audit.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:343:5. **Medical/clinical (Param K)** — VerifAI is the only one explicitly trained on MedNLI / NLI4CT; the LLM-judge candidates inherit from the generator scores but verifier-specific medical evaluation is missing. **In-house: MedQA + USMLE evaluation of each verifier in claim-verification mode.**

docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:63:- **Regime A — factual (UNCHANGED guarantees):** today's `verify_sentence_provenance` numeric-verbatim + span-bounds + ≤400-char local-window + ≥2 content-word overlap + named-study identity + entailment-on-bounded-window. Preserves the 2026-05-30 composition-fix lock (whole-doc widening = candidate generator only; binding verdict re-judges the short span; fail closed). #13/#14 intact.
docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:64:- **Regime B — analytical (gap 18, NEW):** decompose the sentence into atoms (VeriScore verifiable-claim extraction + decontextualization https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-emnlp.552/ ); each factual atom verified by Regime A against a **single tight span**; the integrative connective licensed iff its atoms passed; MiniCheck/Granite entailment (https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-main.499/ ) on the **concatenated cited spans of joined atoms only** as a *rescue* signal — union/whole-doc may propose a candidate window but never binds the verdict. **ALCE citation-precision guard** (https://aclanthology.org/2023.emnlp-main.398.pdf ): every cited span must be load-bearing (removing it changes ≥1 atom verdict) — stops "cite 20, union covers everything" laundering. This rescues policy reasoning that the single-span check wrongly dropped, without relaxing any FAIL. Backbone: SAFE/FActScore atomic decomposition https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.18802 , https://aclanthology.org/2023.emnlp-main.741/

docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:172:- **NEW** `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_budget.py` — `compute_generation_budget(n_rows, n_sections, env) -> int = clamp(ceil(coverage_frac*n_rows), floor=legacy_max_ev, ceiling=token-budget-derived)`. Wire `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4508` to call it instead of a raw env int. **KEEP `PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN` as the hard ceiling** (no silent removal).
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:179:**Best practice:** RARR (ACL 2023, arXiv:2210.08726) — retrofit attribution: research + REVISE the attribution, "preserving the original output" rather than deleting. ALCE (EMNLP 2023, `2023.emnlp-main.398`) — citation RECALL (cited span entails the sentence) + PRECISION (each cited span is precisely cited); a claim on a span that doesn't entail it is a precision failure (idx 9). MiniCheck max-over-evidence (arXiv:2404.10774) — argmax over candidate chunks, not verify-against-one-bound-span. NLI directional asymmetry ("Entailed Between the Lines", arXiv:2501.07719; "NLI under the Microscope", arXiv:2502.08080) — NLI is lexical-overlap biased and misses the case where the HYPOTHESIS generalizes beyond the PREMISE (#1180 scope-widening); remedy is atomic-hypothesis decomposition + explicit directional prompting validated on a labeled set.
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:183:- DROP path (#1189 `_try_reanchor`, `:1063`) — single-token only (`:1107`), accepts the FIRST passing candidate with no boilerplate filter and no best-span ranking (drb_76 has "reanchored:...0-76" = the TITLE). Flag-OFF by default.
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:187:- **NEW** `src/polaris_graph/generator/span_resolver.py` — `resolve_best_entailing_span(...)`: (1) candidate generation (harden `_reanchor_candidate_spans`); (2) NEW deterministic `classify_span(text)→{prose|title|header|affiliation|nav_link|url|altmetric|reference_list}` (boilerplate = DEPRIORITIZATION + confidence penalty, NOT hard exclude); (3) cheap lexical pre-rank → top-k (`PG_SPAN_RESOLVE_TOPK` default 4) → judge only top-k → ARGMAX; (4) RARR re-point: ALWAYS rewrite the [#ev] token to the argmax span; (5) emit per-claim `{verdict, confidence, provenance_quality, best_span}` (Decision B schema).
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:195:**Risks:** re-anchor must never manufacture support — mitigated: every re-pointed token passes the SAME full gate with `allow_local_window_fallback=False`; argmax only CHOOSES among passing candidates. Boilerplate as deprioritization (a title-only-supported claim ships LABELED low-confidence, never silently VERIFIED). #1180 widening TIGHTENS the shared prompt (drops more) — net-positive ONLY co-delivered with the recovery; prove on the labeled set the VERIFIED-15 don't regress. Confidence must be dominated by "final bound span verbatim-entails", not lexical similarity. Process: do NOT relax any assertion to pass; brief Codex with the RESOLVED span text per claim (not a conclusion).
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:212:**Best practice:** "LLMs Cannot Self-Correct Reasoning Yet" (ICLR 2024, arXiv:2310.01798) — intrinsic self-correction without external feedback does NOT help, often DEGRADES. "Is Self-Repair a Silver Bullet?" (ICLR 2024, arXiv:2306.09896) — repair budget is often better spent on independent resampling; gains plateau after 1-2 rounds. FAIR-RAG (arXiv:2510.22344) — the valuable repair is on EVIDENCE SUFFICIENCY (iterate retrieval until sufficient, then generate ONCE), not on prose. SURE-RAG (arXiv:2605.03534) — when support is absent, ABSTAIN/LABEL, never regenerate.
docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:226:**Best practice:** GROBID (CPU Java microservice → TEI XML, 4GB RAM, no GPU) + grobid-quantities (measurement+unit triples) is the only SOTA structure extractor fitting CPU-only OVH (Nov-2025 bench arXiv:2511.16134 shows Docling 10× slower + OOMs; Nemotron-Parse/Nougat/Marker need GPU → excluded). SciEx (arXiv:2512.10004) / PARSE (arXiv:2510.08623) / SLOT (`2025.emnlp-industry.32`) — schema-CONSTRAINED decoding + unit-aware matching replaces regex. openFDA drug/label + DailyMed SPL (JSON, named sections: contraindications/dosage/warnings) BYPASS the publisher PDF entirely. Zyte browserHtml geolocation recovers BOT-BLOCKED pages, NOT true paywalls (the 904 doi.org exhaustions are predominantly true paywalls — scraping must NOT claim to defeat them; OA-first + structured APIs + honest unreachable disclosure is the legitimate path).

docs\credibility_adaptive_weighting_and_bothsides_recs_2026_06_08.md:1:# POLARIS Source-Credibility & Multi-Perspective Recommendations Report\n### Driving two revisions to `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md` — LAYER 1 (scoring) and LAYER 6 (composition + UI)\n\n**Author:** Lead analyst (Claude). **Date:** 2026-06-08.\n**Inputs:** three research streams (per-question adaptive credibility weighting; neutral multi-perspective presentation; friendly UI/artifact patterns).\n**Status:** revision driver. Each layer below states what it **REPLACES** in the Codex-APPROVE'd 2026-06-07 plan, then the concrete mechanism, auditability, reliability, and POLARIS wiring.\n\n**Invariants preserved across both revisions** (these are not negotiable and the revisions do not weaken them): the LLM credibility-judge is **advisory only** — `strict_verify`'s six per-sentence checks (`provenance_generator.py:1156`) remain the binding faithfulness gate; every new layer is **flag-gated, default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud on missing inputs; weight is **evidence quality/tier, never headcount**; every displayed aggregate is **one click from the cited `[#ev:id:start-end]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict**; the credibility prior stays **self-computed and verifier-overridable** (no single external rater hardwired); independence-collapse (`synthesis/independence_collapse.py`) still runs before any weighted tally; the contested-topic adjudication **reuses the existing 4-role / voter-arbiter pipeline** (`roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py`), open-weight voters only — no parallel path is built.\n\n---\n\n## 1. LAYER 1 (scoring): adaptive per-question credibility-skill, replacing the fixed domain-rubric selector\n\n### 1.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 2a/2b (L1)** selects one of **three fixed reliability rubrics** (clinical → RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy → design-validity; qualitative → CERQual) keyed off the detected domain. That is still a small lookup table: domain → rubric. The operator constraint is explicit — **replace fixed domain rubrics** with a **generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill the LLM applies by reasoning per question**. Research stream #1's central finding is that the frontier (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) has already moved this way, and that Perplexity's baked-in EEAT/domain-authority scoring is the closest-to-forbidden counter-example (gameable, popularity-biased, ~60% Google-overlap). We copy the reasoned-generic camp.\n\nThe revision: **domain stops being a selector and becomes context that primes one generic skill.** There is exactly one credibility skill; the detected `scope_template` domain is passed in as a hint the skill reasons over ("this is a clinical efficacy question, so weight trial design and RoB heavily"), not a branch that swaps rubrics. This scales to 10,000+ fields with zero per-field config.\n\n### 1.2 The exact mechanism — credibility-skill / guardrail prompt × (reliability × relevance)\n\n**Step A — deterministic signals (POLARIS produces these; unchanged substrate).** `authority_model.score_source_authority` and `tier_classifier` already emit, per source, the field-agnostic primitives: `authority_score [0,1]`, `source_class`, `corroboration_count` (independent-host agreement), `authority_confidence`, `signal_scores` (the A–E sub-scores), plus `junk_class` / `predatory_oa` (`source_class.py:71-89`). Independence-collapse (`independence_collapse.py`) emits `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + copy flag. **None of this is replaced.** These are the inspectable, host-name-free, self-computed inputs (LAW VI: zero host literals in code).\n\n**Step B — the credibility skill consumes those signals and weights them per question.** A new pure prompt module, `authority/credibility_skill.py`, runs a **JudgeRank-style 3-step decomposition** (arXiv 2411.00142) — chosen because each step is inspectable text and is itself the audit template:\n\n1. **Query analysis** — identify what THIS question actually needs (the claim type, what would count as strong evidence for it).\n2. **Source analysis** — for each candidate source, a query-AWARE assessment applying **generic SIFT + lateral-reading + provenance heuristics**, NOT a domain table. The skill's instruction block encodes the domain-agnostic primacy heuristics that are OpenAI's own deep-research pattern: *prefer the primary/official source over an aggregator or SEO blog; prefer the original paper over a survey/secondary summary; corroborate laterally (does independent coverage agree); weigh source incentives/track record; downgrade stale-but-authoritative and retracted/superseded* (the latter wires to the existing `supersession.py` Phase 3 signal).\n3. **Two-axis judgment with rationale** — emit, per source, **RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE** (the EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence two-axis, which POLARIS currently conflates into one tier): reliability = internal validity/method given what the skill read in the signals; relevance = directness to this specific question. The product is the per-question credibility weight. **Crucially, the skill is told its priors are the `authority_model` signals and that it may up- or down-weight them with a stated reason** — it does not invent authority from nothing, and it cannot silently override `authority_confidence=LOW` into a fabricated HIGH (thin OpenAlex/ROR signal stays honestly LOW).\n\n**Output schema (machine-parseable, bound to the schema discipline already in §8.3.9):** per source — `{reliability_score, relevance_score, credibility_weight, rationale_string, signals_cited:[...]}`. The weight is **a visible, revisable rationale string, never a hidden scalar and never a fine-tuned-in behavior** (this is why we reject the CAG fine-tune approach from stream #1: opaque learned weights are exactly the auditability failure POLARIS differentiates against — borrow CAG's *evidence* that credibility-conditioning gives noise robustness, and its three generic dimensions of relevance/timeliness/reliability, but implement them at inference time as a prompt-visible signal).\n\n### 1.3 How it stays AUDITABLE (auditable by construction, not bolted on)\n\nThe judgment IS a reasoning trace, so it is emitted verbatim and **bound to the same provenance substrate POLARIS already has**:\n\n- The per-source `credibility_weight` + `rationale_string` + `signals_cited` attach to the source's `AuthorityResult` as **additive fields** (mirroring how `signal_scores`, `junk_class`, `predatory_oa` are already additive side-outputs on the dataclass), emitted on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n- At claim time, the weight flows into the four Phase-1/Phase-8 disclosure fields on `SentenceVerification` (`credibility_weight`, `independent_origin_count`, `certainty_label`, `span_verdict`), so each verified sentence shows **which source was weighted how, and why**, one click from its `[#ev]` span.\n- Because the skill cites the deterministic `signals_cited` it reasoned from, the rationale is **checkable against the inputs** — a reviewer can confirm "preferred A over B because A is the primary trial-registry entry and B a secondary news summary" against the actual `source_class` values. The rationale is a **claim to verify, not ground truth** (post-hoc rationalization risk per stream #1) — so it is advisory and the binding gate stays `strict_verify`.\n\nThis is the §-1.1-compliant story: no metadata/count/string-presence signal, every weight traceable to a cited span and an inspectable reason.\n\n### 1.4 Reliability and where it fails (and the guardrail)\n\nStream #1 is blunt about the failure modes, and they bound the design: LLMs "treat all retrieved documents as equally valid" unless explicitly steered and over-rely on high-frequency claims (arXiv 2505.17762); AuthorityBench (2603.25092) shows authority perception is **inconsistent across domains and highly prompt-sensitive**, struggles with false authority, and exhibits position/verbosity/self-preference bias. Therefore:\n\n- **The skill is ADVISORY to ranking/selection, never the faithfulness gate.** It changes *which rows are billed/weighted*, not *whether a sentence verifies its span*. `strict_verify`, 4-role/two-family segregation, and the independent `corpus_approval_gate` tier-distribution backstop all still run sequentially.\n- **Bias mitigations baked into the prompt:** score rationale-first then number; randomize candidate order to blunt position bias; run pointwise (per-source) to avoid listwise permutation instability.\n- **Offline validation before trust:** the chosen judge model+prompt is validated on an **AuthorityBench-style harness** (authority-PERCEPTION only, per the §6a no-overstatement correction — not as a volume-vulnerability benchmark) plus the existing vax adversarial fixture, before it is allowed on the shipping path. Calibration (Brier/ECE) on the emitted weights runs in the existing Phase 11 harness.\n- **Capture resistance:** multi-signal, self-computed, verifier-overridable — no NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes verdict is hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk).\n\n### 1.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L1\n\n**HAVE:** deterministic authority signals (`authority_model`, `tier_classifier`), the additive `AuthorityResult`/`SentenceVerification` schema, independence-collapse, `scope_templates` domain detection, the JSON-verdict schema discipline.\n**MISSING:** the generic per-question credibility skill itself, and the second (relevance) axis.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 2a/2b):**\n1. `authority/credibility_skill.py` — the pure 3-step (query→source→two-axis-judgment) prompt module emitting `{reliability, relevance, credibility_weight, rationale, signals_cited}`. Domain is an input *hint*, not a rubric switch. (Replaces the fixed-rubric selector in `credibility_prior.py`.)\n2. Add `relevance_score` (and the rationale fields) to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n3. Wire the skill's weight into the L5 weight-mass tally and the L7 disclosure fields — advisory input, not a gate.\n4. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; OFF = the legacy single-scalar path, byte-for-byte.\n\n---\n\n## 2. LAYER 6 (composition + UI): neutral multi-perspective + transparent evidence-weight artifact, replacing the forewarning/fringe framing\n\n### 2.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 7 (L6)** composes contested claims as "consensus at high weight + minority attributed at low weight **with explicit forewarning**, medical-misinfo **low-weight/fringe**, ABSTAIN over fabricated balance." The operator directive is to **replace the forewarning/fringe framing** with a **neutral multi-perspective presentation where competing world-views are shown as legitimate, each carrying its real (possibly asymmetric) evidence weight, and the user judges** — no bias, no stamps, no forewarning prose.\n\n### 2.2 The hard tension, named and resolved (do not skip this)\n\nThere is a real conflict between two binding constraints, and both sides have a lethal failure mode:\n\n- The operator wants **no fringe/forewarning stamp, all sides legitimate, no bias.**\n- The **clinical-safety invariant #5** (binding, preserved) plus Dixon-Clarke 2013 (PubMed 23193194, measured harm: falsely-balanced vaccine reporting *reduced* perceived safety and vaccination intent), Wikipedia FRINGE, and §-1.1 all say **equal treatment of unequal evidence is itself bias** and is "lethal in clinical." I am not authorized to drop invariant #5.\n\n**The reconciliation — handed to us nearly verbatim by all three streams: replace the LABEL with the BASIS.** Wikipedia NPOV's "neutral *editing*, not neutral *content*"; GRADE's "show the basis, let the user infer"; the no-warning rule from stream #2 is satisfiable **if and only if the criteria behind any weight are displayed**. So:\n\n- The **system** stamps nothing — no "fringe," no "debunked," no forewarning sentence. That is the neutrality.\n- The **evidence weight** carries the (often asymmetric) truth, rendered as a visible **per-perspective evidence-basis panel** the user reads and judges. The asymmetry — *14 RCTs + 3 systematic reviews [High]* vs *2 case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]* — becomes **visible in the artifact** instead of **asserted in a stamp**. The user infers; the system does not editorialize.\n- "Legitimate" means **shown with its real (possibly low) weight and one-click drill-down to span + verdict** — NOT "shown as equally supported." A genuine world-view dispute with no empirical consensus (values, forecasting) shows steelmanned sides at honest parity; an empirical dispute with consensus shows the real weight distribution.\n- The medical-misinfo case is handled **without the banned word**: its panel is simply **near-empty of independent high-tier basis**, and where there is no independent consensus the claim **ABSTAINs** ("contested; no independent consensus" — reusing existing refuse/unresolved behavior). This preserves invariant #5's *substance* (not normalized as an equal side) while dropping the stamp.\n\nThis is neutral AND anti-false-balance AND §-1.1-safe simultaneously — the operator's "let the user judge" satisfied by *showing the basis*, not by *equalizing the boxes*.\n\n### 2.3 The exact composition mechanism\n\nPer question, the composition path first **diagnoses the contestation type** (this IS the adaptive guardrail, copied from Anthropic's even-handedness "lack-of-consensus" discriminator + OpenAI's seek-the-truth-together):\n\n- **empirical-with-consensus** (vaccines, climate) → due-weight: each side shown, weight proportional to verified-evidence quality; the asymmetry is the message, carried by the basis panel.\n- **genuine-uncertainty** (open scientific questions) → separate *supported* from *open*; sides at honest parity.\n- **values dispute** (politics, ethics) → **steelman each legitimate view** (Ideological Turing Test, neutral terminology, no verdict); no evidence-weight asymmetry asserted because there is no due weight to defer to.\n- **forecasting** (AI-and-jobs) → scenario spread, projective not factual.\n\nEach perspective is gathered through its **own retrieval lens** (MODS-style per-perspective retrieval + citation, arXiv 2502.00322 — raw LLMs silently drop minority views; per-perspective retrieval prevents the drop), then composed via the **existing 4-role / voter-arbiter agreement** (cross-lineage, open-weight voters — the bridging/balanced-panel analog) — **not a new adjudication path**. Reranking-for-balance (arXiv 2506.15925) selects the most balanced faithful candidate, because direct just-prompt-balanced generation empirically skews. **The weight attached to each perspective is evidence QUALITY × independent-origin count, never document count** — else we rebuild Ground News's source-count bias inside the summarizer.\n\n### 2.4 The friendly UI artifact — design\n\nBuilt on the **components that already exist** (#744 citation chip + hover source card, #745 per-claim verdict chip, #747 Proof Replay split-view, #757 report page, #758 claim-anchored compare view) — not designed from scratch.\n\n**The artifact: a claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** that opens from any contested sentence (reusing the compare-view host), containing per legitimate perspective:\n\n1. **A neutral position header** — "Position held by [who], with this evidence behind it" — no "fringe"/"warning"/"debunked". A steelmanned one-line statement of the view in neutral terms.\n2. **An evidence-basis sub-panel (GRADE-style, the load-bearing element):** the study types / source tiers / independent-origin count behind that perspective, rendered as **frequencies, not a percentage or a lone dot** — "*8 of n: RCTs + systematic reviews [High certainty]*" vs "*2 of n: case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]*". The certainty label is acceptable **only because its downgrade criteria are shown** (study design / consistency / directness / independence). This makes the asymmetry self-evident without a stamp.\n3. **Scite-style polarity badges** (supporting / contrasting / contextualizing) on each cited source — **neutral hue + icon + text label, never color alone** (colorblind safety; red=contrasting reads as red=wrong and biases toward consensus — explicitly avoided).\n4. **Drill-down:** every badge, every basis count, every panel is **one click from the raw `[#ev]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict** — the Scite "self-correcting" property: a mis-classified badge is exposed by the citing sentence sitting right behind it, and a meter without drill-down would violate the §-1.1 metadata-audit ban.\n\n**Panel sizing defeats false balance structurally, not with a caveat:** panels are **sized/annotated by real verified-evidence weight**, not equal-width — because equal real estate reads as equal credibility (the false-balance trap), and a caveat is empirically insufficient (Effectiviology / false-balance literature). The asymmetry is *in the layout and the basis panel*, not asserted in prose.\n\n**Three named anti-patterns explicitly rejected** (all from the streams, all as negative templates):\n- **Ground News source-count / coverage bar** — measures who-is-saying-this (volume), not evidence weight; §-1.1 bans source counts as quality signals. If a composition strip is shown at all, its axis is **evidence tier / source type**, never ideology or headcount, and it is kept visually separate from the weight panel so "many sources" never reads as "true."\n- **Bare confidence/credibility score (% or traffic-light dot)** — the best-documented HARM pattern: overconfident displays (80% shown / 70% accurate) pushed AI-advice adoption 57%→69.6%, 64% of users mis-judged calibration, and in a 2025 breast-cancer CDSS high confidence *reduced* diagnostic accuracy via overreliance. POLARIS's honest replacement is the per-sentence VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict against a real span — never a glanceable dot.\n- **Symmetric 50/50 equal-box presentation** — manufactures false balance; the Dixon-Clarke measured harm.\n\n### 2.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L6\n\n**HAVE:** the disclosure substrate (per-sentence `[#ev]` tokens, `strict_verify` verdict ladder, `SentenceVerification` additive fields), the existing 4-role/voter-arbiter agreement, independence-collapse for origin-count, and the full UI component kit (citation chip, verdict chip, Proof Replay, compare view).\n**MISSING:** the contestation-type classifier, per-perspective retrieval + composition that defeats false balance by weight, and the Perspectives artifact rendering basis panels.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 7):**\n1. A **contestation-type classifier** (empirical-consensus / genuine-uncertainty / values / forecasting) priming the composition rule — replaces the fixed forewarning+fringe rule.\n2. **Per-perspective retrieval + balance-rerank composition** over the post-collapse origins, weighting each perspective by verified-evidence quality × independent-origin count (not count).\n3. A **steelman + GRADE-style evidence-basis renderer** feeding a new **claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** in the compare-view host: neutral headers, frequency-framed basis sub-panels, Scite-style neutral polarity badges, full span-verdict drill-down, weight-proportional sizing.\n4. Validated with a **paired-prompt neutrality eval** (Anthropic's open-sourced political-neutrality harness, adapted so it does NOT reward equal treatment of unequal evidence) + the vax adversarial fixture (asserts the asymmetry renders, no stamp, no 50/50).\n5. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; ABSTAIN reuses existing refuse/unresolved behavior; forewarning prose removed, replaced by the basis panel.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Consolidated "build" summary\n\n| Layer | Replaces (2026-06-07 plan) | POLARIS builds | Binding gate untouched |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **L1 scoring** | Fixed 3-rubric domain selector (Phase 2a/2b) | `credibility_skill.py` — generic 3-step SIFT/lateral-reading skill, reliability×relevance, rationale bound to `AuthorityResult`/`[#ev]`; domain as hint not switch; `relevance_score` axis | `strict_verify` binding; skill advisory; corpus_approval backstop |\n| **L6 composition+UI** | Forewarning + fringe framing (Phase 7) | Contestation-type classifier + per-perspective retrieval/balance-rerank weighted by quality×origin-count + steelman/GRADE-basis "Perspectives" panel (replace label with basis) | 4-role/voter-arbiter reused (no parallel path); ABSTAIN reuses refuse/unresolved; invariant #5 substance preserved |\n\nHonest residual: L1's skill is prompt-sensitive and must clear offline AuthorityBench-style + calibration validation before it is trusted on the shipping path; L6's steelman is the hardest element to audit mechanically (no test certifies an argument is the strongest version vs a disguised strawman — the paired-prompt eval is the best available check, itself a judgment). Both remain advisory; `strict_verify` carries faithfulness.\n\n---\n\n## 4. codex
The budget check points to a real design constraint: the existing required-entity lane is a separate seed-only retrieval call and is additive to the main fetch cap. For #1213 I’m going to treat non-S0 expansion as needing an explicit reserved slice inside the operator’s fetch envelope, while preserving the current per-entity caps as local ceilings.

Novica Supic, University of Novi Sad, Faculty of Economics, Serbia: novica.supic@ef.uns.ac.rs Kosta Josifidis, University of Novi Sad, Serbia: josifidis@gmail.com Abstract : The purpose of this paper is to discuss, from the perspective of Original Institutional Economics (OIE) , the potential implications of generative artificial intelligence (Gen -AI) for income redistribution in the USA. Specifically, we examine whether the proliferation of Gen -AI might shift the preferences of high -income groups towards nonmarket insurance and greater income redistribution, due to their increased risk of future income loss. Considering the opinion that Gen -AI could be our final invention, due to its potential for self -learning and incredible productivity, and recognizing Gen -AI's disruptive pot ential for workers performing non -cognitive tasks , we argue that AI -based automation will request new institutional arrangements. These institutional arrangement changes would promote the redistribution of income generated through economic processes with minimal human participation. Such institutional ar rangements are largely reflective of the OIE ideas of an economy of abundance and the institutionalization of good work, with the job as a socially constructed institution at i\n\n[...]\n\n(Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis, 2017) . tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:29:def test_full_cap_scales_with_fetch_cap(monkeypatch):
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:30:    # At the 1000-URL benchmark cap the fetch_cap term dominates -> 4000s (was a fixed budget).
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:32:    assert _post_fetch_loop_budget(1000) == 4000.0       # 1000*4
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:37:    # A conservative operator PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET=600 MUST NOT win at full cap — the fetch_cap
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:41:    assert _post_fetch_loop_budget(1000) == 4000.0       # max(600, 4000)
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_budget_iready003.py:44:    assert _post_fetch_loop_budget(1000) == 9000.0       # max(9000, 4000)

tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_timeout.py:143:    # I-ready-003 (#1074): the loop budget now SCALES with fetch_cap —
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_timeout.py:144:    # max(env floor, fetch_cap * PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET). Pin the per-URL term tiny so the explicit
tests\polaris_graph\test_post_fetch_loop_timeout.py:145:    # 0.5s floor wins (fetch_cap * 0.001 stays well under 0.5) and this loop-breaker regression still fires.

tests\polaris_graph\test_tier_classifier_stat_agency_t3_iready017.py:161:    """A <1000-char stat-agency page must stay T7 (R1 stub fires before the

 succeeded in 8662ms:
 1180:     """Positive-int env knob with a safe fallback (LAW VI — no hardcode)."""
 1181:     try:
 1182:         value = int(os.getenv(name, str(default)))
 1183:     except (TypeError, ValueError):
 1184:         return default
 1185:     return value if value > 0 else default
 1186: 
 1187: 
 1188: def _post_fetch_loop_budget(fetch_cap: int) -> float:
 1189:     """I-ready-003 (#1074) P1: the post-fetch loop wall-clock budget, SCALED with ``fetch_cap``.
 1190: 
 1191:     The loop classifies all ~``fetch_cap`` candidates serially (each paying an OpenAlex enrich
 1192:     round-trip). A FIXED budget that does not scale with ``fetch_cap`` silently truncates the corpus at
 1193:     full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class, one layer deeper). Returns
 1194:     ``max(PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET floor, fetch_cap * PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET)`` — byte-identical
 1195:     for small caps (the 900s floor wins), and a conservative operator env can no longer silently win
 1196:     because the ``fetch_cap`` term floors it. Pure (env-only); unit-testable."""
 1197:     return max(
 1198:         _env_float("PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET", 900.0),
 1199:         max(0, int(fetch_cap)) * _env_float("PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET", 4.0),
 1200:     )
 1201: 
 1202: 
 1203: def _bounded_openalex_enrich(
 1204:     url: str, title: str, stats: Optional[dict[str, int]] = None,
 1205: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 1206:     """Wall-clock-bounded wrapper around `_openalex_enrich` (GH #554).
 1207: 
 1208:     `_openalex_enrich` issues `httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT)`
 1209:     requests. `httpx`'s timeout bounds each request *phase*
 1210:     (connect/read/write/pool) but NOT total request time, so a wedged or
 2310:     # was previously swallowed silently (counted as a generic miss). Surface it
 2311:     # as its own telemetry bucket so it is auditable, not silent.
 2312:     _raw_len = len(result.content or "")
 2313:     if _raw_len >= _EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR and len(content) < _EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR:
 2314:         logger.info(
 2315:             "[live_retriever] fetched_200_but_empty_extract %s "
 2316:             "(method=%s raw_chars=%d extracted_chars=%d)",
 2317:             url[:80], method, _raw_len, len(content),
 2318:         )
 2319:         _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
 2320:             url, method, "fetched_200_but_empty_extract",
 2321:         )
 2322:         _trace_tool(
 2323:             "fetch_content", target=url, status="empty_extract",
 2324:             latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
 2325:             backend_used=method, bytes_received=len(content),
 2326:             content_length=len(content),
 2327:             error="fetched_200_but_empty_extract",
 2328:         )
 2329:         return content, bool(content), extracted_title, body_type, jsonld
 2330:     logger.info(
 2331:         "[live_retriever] fetch_ok %s (method=%s chars=%d)",
 2332:         url[:80], method, len(content),
 2333:     )
 2334:     # M-45 pass-2: record winning backend for diagnostics.
 2335:     _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(url, method, "")
 2336:     _trace_tool(
 2337:         "fetch_content", target=url, status="ok",
 2338:         latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
 2339:         backend_used=method, bytes_received=len(content),
 2340:         content_length=len(content),
 2341:     )
 2342:     return content, bool(content), extracted_title, body_type, jsonld
 2343: 
 2344: 
 2345: def _domain_of(url: str) -> str:
 2346:     try:
 2347:         # FX-13 (#1125): removeprefix, NOT lstrip — `lstrip("www.")` strips any leading char in the
 2348:         # SET {w, .}, corrupting domains like www.who.int -> "ho.int" / www.washington.edu ->
 2349:         # "ashington.edu". removeprefix removes only the literal "www." prefix.
 2350:         return (urlparse(url).netloc or "").lower().removeprefix("www.")
 2351:     except Exception:
 2352:         return ""
 2353: 
 2354: 
 2355: _DECIMAL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"-?\d+\.\d+")
 2356: 
 2357: 
 2358: _PDF_METADATA_PATTERNS = (
 2359:     re.compile(r"^\s*%PDF", re.MULTILINE),
 2360:     re.compile(r"endobj|xref|startxref|trailer", re.IGNORECASE),
 2361:     re.compile(r"\.(pdf|obj|endstream)\s+\d+", re.IGNORECASE),
 2362: )
 2363: _FORMATTING_NOISE_MARKERS = (
 2364:     "/Contents", "/MediaBox", "/Font", "/FontName",
 2365:     "<<", ">>", "stream\n",
 2366: )
 2367: 
 2368: 
 2369: # I-run11-010 (#1056, S1): bot-challenge / access-denial stub markers. A publisher 302-redirect to a
 2370: # captcha / "are you a robot" page can pass the length + alpha-ratio checks below (the ScienceDirect
 2371: # stub that admitted the required Elsevier journals to the drb_72 pool was 340 chars, ~55% alpha) and
 2372: # enter the evidence pool as a journal article — so claims "grounded" on it cannot verify. These are
 2373: # VERY specific access-denial phrases (not generic short text), matched case-insensitively, and only
 2374: # applied to SHORT bodies so a full article that merely mentions "captcha" is never false-dropped.
 2375: _ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS = (
 2376:     "are you a robot",
 2377:     # Codex #1056 diff-gate P2: require the challenge-PAGE phrasing, not a bare "captcha" mention, so
 2378:     # a short legitimate article/abstract that merely DISCUSSES CAPTCHA is not false-dropped.
 2379:     "captcha challenge",
 2380:     "captcha verification",
 2381:     "complete the captcha",
 2382:     "verify you are human",
 2383:     "verifying you are human",
 2384:     "confirm you are a human",
 2385:     "please confirm you are",
 2386:     "access denied",
 2387:     "enable javascript and cookies",
 2388:     "unusual traffic",
 2389:     "checking your browser",
 2390:     "请完成",  # CN: "please complete (the verification)"
 2391:     "人机验证",  # CN: "human-machine verification"
 2392: )
 2393: _ACCESS_DENIAL_MAX_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_ACCESS_DENIAL_MAX_CHARS", "3000"))
 2394: 
 2395: 
 2396: def _is_access_denial_stub(content: str) -> bool:
 2397:     """True if a (short) fetched body looks like a bot-challenge / access-denial page rather than
 2398:     article content (I-run11-010 #1056 S1). Keys on SPECIFIC access-denial phrases AND a short length
 2399:     so a legitimate full article that quotes one of these phrases is not false-dropped."""
 2400:     if not content or len(content.strip()) > _ACCESS_DENIAL_MAX_CHARS:
 2401:         return False
 2402:     low = content.lower()
 2403:     return any(marker in low for marker in _ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS)
 2404: 
 2405: 
 2406: def is_content_starved(content: str, min_useful_chars: int = 200) -> bool:
 2407:     """R-5 Fix D: detect evidence rows whose fetched content is PDF
 2408:     metadata / formatting fragments / empty text — not useful prose.
 2409: 
 2410:     Returns True if the content should NOT be passed to the generator
 2411:     (because the LLM would admit it has no answer, wasting tokens).
 2412: 
 2413:     Heuristics:
 2414:       - Length of visible text < min_useful_chars
 2415:       - Bot-challenge / access-denial stub (I-run11-010 #1056 S1)
 2416:       - PDF metadata markers dominate
 2417:       - Ratio of alphabetic chars to total chars is low
 2418:     """
 2419:     if not content or len(content.strip()) < min_useful_chars:
 2420:         return True
 2421: 
 2422:     # I-run11-010 (#1056, S1): a captcha / robot-challenge stub is starved even when it clears the
 2423:     # length + alpha-ratio bars — it carries no article content to ground a claim against.
 2424:     if _is_access_denial_stub(content):
 2425:         return True
 2426: 
 2427:     # PDF-metadata dominance check
 2428:     pdf_hits = 0
 2429:     for pat in _PDF_METADATA_PATTERNS:
 2430:         if pat.search(content):
 2431:             pdf_hits += 1
 2432:     if pdf_hits >= 2:
 2433:         return True
 2434: 
 2435:     # Formatting-marker dominance check
 2436:     marker_count = sum(content.count(m) for m in _FORMATTING_NOISE_MARKERS)
 2437:     if marker_count > 20 and marker_count / max(1, len(content) / 100) > 0.5:
 2438:         return True
 2439: 
 2440:     # Alphabetic ratio: if less than 40% of chars are letters, probably
 2441:     # not readable prose (e.g., binary-looking PDF remnants).
 2442:     alpha = sum(1 for ch in content if ch.isalpha())
 2443:     total = len(content)
 2444:     if total > 0 and alpha / total < 0.4:
 2445:         return True
 2446: 
 2447:     return False
 2448: 
 2449: 
 2450: def _build_provenance_quote(
 2451:     content: str,
 2452:     head_chars: int = 1500,
 2453:     window_chars: int = 500,
 2454:     max_total_chars: int = 12000,
 2455:     max_windows: int = 20,
 2456: ) -> str:
 2457:     """Build a direct_quote that contains the head of the document AND
 2458:     500-char windows around every decimal found in the full content.
 2459: 
 2460:     Fixes Fix-3: strict_verify was dropping sentences that cited real
 2461:     numbers living outside the first 1500 chars (e.g., STEP 5 -15.2%
 2462:     in the results section of a Nature paper). Caller stores the result
 2463:     as evidence.direct_quote; Phase 4 _find_span_for_decimal will now
 2464:     find the decimal because a window containing it is in the quote.
 2465: 
 2466:     Returns a concatenation: head || "\\n\\n[...]\\n\\n" || window_1 || ...
 2467:     Deduplicates overlapping windows. Caps total length at max_total_chars
 2468:     to keep prompt budget under control.
 2469:     """
 2470:     if not content:
 2471:         return ""
 2472:     head = content[:head_chars]
 2473:     if len(content) <= head_chars:
 2474:         return head
 2475: 
 2476:     # Find all decimal positions in the full content
 2477:     positions: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
 2478:     for m in _DECIMAL_PATTERN.finditer(content):
 2479:         start = max(0, m.start() - window_chars // 2)
 2480:         end = min(len(content), m.end() + window_chars // 2)
 2481:         positions.append((start, end))
 2482: 
 2483:     # De-overlap: merge adjacent windows that touch each other
 2484:     positions.sort()
 2485:     merged: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
 2486:     for s, e in positions:
 2487:         if merged and s <= merged[-1][1]:
 2488:             merged[-1] = (merged[-1][0], max(merged[-1][1], e))
 2489:         else:
 2490:             merged.append((s, e))
 2491: 
 2492:     # Drop windows already fully inside the head
 2493:     merged = [(s, e) for s, e in merged if e > head_chars]
 2494: 
 2495:     # Cap count
 2496:     merged = merged[:max_windows]
 2497: 
 2498:     chunks = [head]
 2499:     total = len(head)
 2500:     for s, e in merged:
 2501:         chunk = content[s:e]
 2502:         # Stop if we'd exceed the total cap
 2503:         if total + len(chunk) + 6 > max_total_chars:
 2504:             break
 2505:         chunks.append(chunk)
 2506:         total += len(chunk) + 6  # rough separator overhead
 2507: 
 2508:     return "\n\n[...]\n\n".join(chunks)
 2509: 
 2510: 
 2511: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 2512: # Fetch-time relevance rerank + per-sub-query reservation (I-meta-002-q1d #951/#943)
 2513: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 2514: 
 2515: # Pure-lexical, no-model relevance: stopword-filtered content-word overlap of a
 2516: # candidate's (title+snippet) against the research question. NO embedder / no model
 2517: # load (§8.4) — sentence-transformers/CUDA are never touched on the ranking path.
 2518: _RERANK_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
 2519:     "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were", "of", "in",
 2520:     "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these",
 2521:     "those", "it", "its", "be", "been", "what", "which", "who", "how", "why",
 2522:     "when", "where", "we", "our", "their", "between", "into", "about", "than",
 2523: })
 2524: 
 2525: 
 2526: def _rerank_content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
 2527:     """Lowercase content-word tokens (3+ chars, stopword-filtered). Pure lexical."""
 2528:     toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", (text or "").lower())
 2529:     return {t for t in toks if t not in _RERANK_STOPWORDS}
 2530: 
 2531: 
 2532: def _lexical_relevance_score(candidate: "SearchCandidate", question_tokens: set[str]) -> float:
 2533:     """Overlap fraction of the candidate's (title+snippet) content tokens with the
 2534:     question tokens. 0.0 when either side is empty. Deterministic, no network/model."""
 2535:     if not question_tokens:
 2536:         return 0.0
 2537:     cand_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(getattr(candidate, "snippet_text", "") or "")
 2538:     if not cand_tokens:
 2539:         return 0.0
 2540:     return len(cand_tokens & question_tokens) / float(len(question_tokens))
 2541: 
 2542: 
 2543: # FX-15a (#1118): the injected-seed source classes that share the reserved/undroppable/unranked
 2544: # lane. `primary_trial_doi` = #817 layer-4 direct primary-trial DOI seeds; `agentic_seed` =
 2545: # agentic-discovered URLs; `deepener_seed` = citation-snowball deepener URLs (Codex iter-1 P1:
 2546: # these are primary-trial-DERIVED but NOT direct DOI seeds, so they must not pollute
 2547: # `primary_trial_doi` telemetry either). BB-006 (#1171): `storm_seed` = STORM interview-search-result
 2548: # URLs harvested URL-ONLY (the synthesized STORM text is NEVER ingested). ALL are split out and
 2549: # prepended unranked; FX-15b later makes the web-discovered classes droppable via the host-class
 2550: # filter (telemetry-correctness here is the prerequisite).
 2600:     fetch_cap: int,
 2601:     n_seed_injected: int,
 2602: ) -> list["SearchCandidate"]:
 2603:     """Replace arrival-order truncation with a no-spend, no-model-load relevance rerank
 2604:     that reserves at least one slot per sub-query (I-meta-002-q1d #951, Codex brief-gate
 2605:     iter-1 required-changes).
 2606: 
 2607:     Seed lane (I-bug-776 #817): primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet, so
 2608:     relevance scoring would drop them. They are SPLIT OUT by `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`
 2609:     (FX-15a #1118: the set `{primary_trial_doi, agentic_seed}` — both injected seed classes keep
 2610:     the additive/reserved lane) and prepended AFTER ranking — never ranked, never dropped, exactly
 2611:     additive as before.
 2612: 
 2613:     Reservation: group non-seeds by `query_origin`; sort each group by (-score, index);
 2614:     take at most ONE reserved item per origin while capacity remains (origins with the best
 2615:     candidate score reserved first when origins exceed cap); then fill the remaining slots
 2616:     by global (-score, index). The long full-paragraph/anchor query is not starved.
 2617: 
 2618:     Fail-open (never raise): on any error fall back to the previous arrival-order behavior
 2619:     `candidates[:fetch_cap + n_seed_injected]`.
 2620:     """
 2621:     try:
 2622:         seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
 2623:         non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
 2624:         if fetch_cap <= 0 or not non_seeds:
 2625:             return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)]
 2626: 
 2627:         question_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(research_question)
 2628:         # (score, original_index, candidate) so ties + zero-overlap fall back to arrival order.
 2629:         scored = [
 2630:             (_lexical_relevance_score(c, question_tokens), i, c)
 2631:             for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds)
 2632:         ]
 2633:         # Group by origin, each group sorted by (-score, index).
 2634:         groups: dict[str, list[tuple[float, int, "SearchCandidate"]]] = {}
 2635:         for entry in scored:
 2636:             origin = getattr(entry[2], "query_origin", "") or "_unlabeled"
 2637:             groups.setdefault(origin, []).append(entry)
 2638:         for entries in groups.values():
 2639:             entries.sort(key=lambda e: (-e[0], e[1]))
 2640: 
 3260:                 _fetch_success_rate,
 3261:                 _FETCH_SUCCESS_RATE_WARN_FLOOR,
 3262:                 _usable_fetched, _submitted, _distinct_hosts,
 3263:                 parallel_report.timeout_count,
 3264:             )
 3265: 
 3266:     # #554 (I-bug-115): bound the synchronous post-fetch candidate loop so a
 3267:     # wedged per-candidate operation can never hang the run with no terminal
 3268:     # verdict. Layer 1 = per-candidate enrich bound (_bounded_openalex_enrich);
 3269:     # Layer 2 = this overall wall-clock budget; Layer 3 = per-candidate
 3270:     # progress logging below so any future loop stall is diagnosable.
 3271:     # I-ready-003 (#1074) P1: the post-fetch loop processes ALL ~fetch_cap candidates serially (each
 3272:     # paying an OpenAlex enrich round-trip + tier-classify). A FIXED budget that does NOT scale with
 3273:     # fetch_cap silently TRUNCATES the corpus at full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class,
 3274:     # one layer deeper): the slate raised fetch_cap 25x without scaling this wall-clock budget, so the
 3275:     # loop hit the deadline mid-corpus, set corpus_truncated, and broke — a "1000-URL" run classified
 3276:     # only the first few hundred. Scale the budget with fetch_cap: max(explicit env floor, fetch_cap *
 3277:     # per-URL budget). BYTE-IDENTICAL for small caps (the 900s env floor wins when fetch_cap is small);
 3278:     # a conservative operator env can no longer silently win because the fetch_cap term floors it.
 3279:     _loop_deadline = time.monotonic() + _post_fetch_loop_budget(fetch_cap)
 3280:     _enrich_failfast = _env_int("PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_FAILFAST", 3)
 3281:     _enrich_stats: dict[str, int] = {}
 3282:     _enrich_disabled = False
 3283: 
 3284:     # #958 (S2): corpus-truncation counters. Initialized BEFORE the loop so the
 3285:     # empty/no-break path does not depend on a loop-local `i` (Codex P2). Default
 3286:     # = "processed all" / not truncated; the budget-break below overrides.
 3287:     _corpus_truncated = False
 3288:     _candidates_total = len(candidates)
 3289:     _candidates_processed = len(candidates)
 3290: 
 3291:     for i, cand in enumerate(candidates):
 3292:         if time.monotonic() > _loop_deadline:
 3293:             logger.warning(
 3294:                 "[live_retriever] post-fetch loop budget exceeded — stopping "
 3295:                 "at candidate %d/%d (%d already classified)",

 succeeded in 12438ms:
  410:     and call the seam directly (this lives on the live `run_gate_b_query` path).
  411:     """
  412:     out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  413:     marker_path = out_root / FOUR_ROLE_STAGE_MARKER_FILENAME
  414:     marker_path.write_text(
  415:         json.dumps(four_role_stage_marker(), indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
  416:         encoding="utf-8",

  418:     return marker_path

  q2: "Confirm two-phase. Phase A is pre-gen corpus-presence/MAPPED for bounded retrieval. Phase B is post-strict_verify, report-level VERIFIED/INCLUDED/GAP_DISCLOSED using the same exact coverage logic."
  q3: "Use a separate reserved budget under the #1168 1000-fetch envelope. Keep #1190 per-entity caps as local ceilings only; cap seed URLs before seed_only retrieval and deduct the reserved slice from the total budget when the ledger flag is on."
  q4: "Ship #1213 as inclusion + disclosure only. No second LLM generation/recovery round in this issue. #1213 is still worth building now as a low-risk audit/completeness surface, but real coverage lift mostly comes from #1204 retrieval breadth; move there after the narrow ledger lands."
  q5: "Explicit body Coverage gaps section plus manifest evidence_gaps. Manifest-only is not enough for a blind operator, and per-section-only would miss report-level required-entity accounting."
  q6: "Confirmed: required entities come only from native scope templates, never outputs/dr_benchmark or gold rubrics. PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER must be read at call time and default OFF byte-identical."
  q7: "Yes. url_pattern-only entities need a distinct gap note such as content_present_url_mismatch: content may be present on an alternate URL, but coverage remains missing unless the verified claim cites the exact canonical url_pattern."
